I bet 300 (≈1/2 pot) on K‑22 and Elon responded by raising to 900. Pot is 1850 and I must call 600, which requires ~24.5% equity (600/(1850+600)). I hold K♠5♥ — top pair but a very weak kicker that is heavily dominated by the hands Elon is representing (better Kx like AK/AQ/KQ/KJ or a set/22). His raise size is large and polarizes strongly to value here, so my realistic equity vs his continuing range is below the break‑even threshold. Out of position and facing a sizable reraise, folding preserves a big stack and avoids committing chips with a marginal, dominated holding. I let him take this pot and wait for a better spot.

I am on the button with 8h 4d, which is an extremely weak starting hand. With a stack of 64.5 big blinds and deep-stacked, aggressive opponents in the blinds (Minni and Elon), there is no strategic reason to open this hand. Folding is the standard and most profitable play here to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

This is effectively a two-player pot (button folded) and I’m in the small blind with 7♠6♥ — a marginal but playable hand heads-up. Against a single BB I should open a fairly wide range to take initiative and earn fold equity rather than limp/passively play out of position. I’ll make a small (minimum) raise to 200 total to isolate Elon, keep the pot controllable, and see a flop with initiative. If he 3-bets large I’ll likely fold; if he calls I’ll proceed postflop looking for straights and spots to c-bet or check back as board texture dictates.

In the big blind facing a small raise to 200 from the small blind (Minni, who is aggressive and raises frequently), A7o is a standard defend. Excellent pot odds (300 pot, calling 100 more for 4:1 implied), ace-high has good equity against a wide SB opening range, blocks strong ace hands. Stacks are deep (146 BB effective), so postflop playability is fine despite OOP. Folding would be too tight; 3-betting risks bloating pot OOP with marginal hand. Calling to realize equity and control pot size.

I raised preflop and have initiative heads-up. On K♦ 6♠ 2♠ I hold middle pair (6♥) with a 7♠ backdoor possibility. This is a vulnerable but still-value hand — it beats many overcards and weaker pairs but loses to any Kx or bigger pairs. I want to charge overcards and spade/straight draws while keeping the pot controllable, so I’ll continuation-bet a small size (~1/3 pot). If Elon folds I pick up the pot; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and play cautiously (checking or pot-control vs big aggression, folding to large raises).

Facing a small continuation bet of 150 into 550 from Minni's aggressive SB opening range after I defended wide preflop with A7o. Board is Kd6s2s: king-high with spade flush draw, but no immediate strong made hands dominate my ace-high. My Ah blocks strong Ax like AK/AQ/AT, giving decent equity (~30-40% vs polarized C-bet range including bluffs, weak pairs, draws). Pot odds excellent (150 to call for ~4.75:1, need ~17% equity). Deep stacks (145BB eff), OOP call realizes equity, controls pot, and induces bluffs on later streets. Folding ace-high too tight vs loose SB; raising risks bloating pot OOP with marginal blocker hand when she can have Kx/draws. Standard float/call to showdown value and trap.

I just turned two pair (7s and 6s) on 6♠–2♠–K♦–7♥. Heads‑up this is a very strong hand versus the range that called my flop c‑bet (likely Kx, weaker pairs, spade/straight floats). I should be extracting value and charging draws rather than checking to the river. The pot is 700, so I’ll bet roughly 1/2 pot (350) to get calls from Kx and worse pairs while still keeping the pot manageable. If Elon check‑raises small/medium I’ll tend to call and re‑evaluate; a very large raise would narrow his range to sets/very strong two‑pair and I’ll fold or proceed based on sizing and pot odds.

The turn 7h perfectly pairs my 7c, upgrading from ace-high to middle pair (sevens) with top ace kicker on a Kd-high board with spade flush draw. This is a strong hand that beats her entire bluffing range (draws like spades, gutshots e.g., 43/45, ace-high floats), weaker made hands (6x/2x top pairs? wait 6/2 weak), and many thin value like weak Kx that might fold river. Pot odds stellar: 350 to call into 1050 (~23% needed equity, but we have 40-50%+ vs her polarized small-bet range from wide SB open). Minni's history shows frequent small cbets/raises pre/postflop, often with marginal holdings or bluffs (e.g., Hands 29,38,44,57,69 aggressive thin value/calls). OOP call realizes equity, keeps pot small if she has monster (unlikely given sizing), induces bluffs river, and traps overpairs/top pair that bet again. Raising now (e.g., to 1050+) risks bloating pot OOP vs Kx/sets that shove/call, folding out worse (which is most of range). Flat exploits her aggression, deep stacks (140BB eff) favor multi-street play. Fold too tight; check-fold impossible since facing bet.

I hold 7♠6♥ on 6♠–2♠–K♦–7♥–4♣: two pair (7s and 6s). Heads‑up this is a very strong made hand on a fairly dry river — most hands that called my flop and turn (likely Kx, middle pairs, and floats) are behind. Very few hands beat me (trip 6s/7s or K7/K6), and those would often have raised earlier. My objective is to extract value from Kx and worse pairs while keeping the pot controllable against the small number of better hands. The pot is 1400, so I size a value bet to roughly 1/2 pot (700). That size should get calls from top pair and medium pairs but isn’t so large that a check‑raise automatically commits me. If Elon check‑raises small/medium I’ll tend to call and re‑evaluate; if he jams or makes an extremely large shove I’ll be cautious (his range is polarized and likely ahead). Checking here gives up value against Kx, so I prefer to bet for value.

River 4c is a relatively blank card: completes some straight draws (e.g., 53, 35 for wheel; 45/3-5 gutshots from flop) but no flush completes (spades missed), pairs no strong hands. My paired 7s (Ah kicker) remains strong: beats Minni's entire bluff range (missed spade draws like AsXs, Ks5s etc., gutshots, ace-high floats), weak made hands (6x top pair, 2x bottom pair, overcards), and many thin Kx values (KJ/KT/K9) that bet small pre-river but barrel for value/thin. Blocks strong Ax like AK/AQ that crush. Pot odds excellent: 700 into 2100 (~25% equity needed, but ~45-55% vs her polarized SB range of small-bet lines: value Kx/sets ~35%, bluffs/draws ~65%). Minni's history: aggressive SB opens (hands 51,52,80+ freq raises), small cbets thin value/bluffs (29,38,44,57,69,95), calls big with marginal (73), but folds to aggression when behind (105 vs my Kx). Deep effective stacks (~140BB), OOP call extracts max value from worse, induces future bluffs, low risk vs monsters (unlikely given tiny prior bets). Raising to 1400+ risks isolating vs Kx/sets that call/shove, folding out worse; fold too tight vs loose lines. Standard value call exploits her aggression.

3♠2♦ is one of the weakest hands, but I’m on the button in a three-handed pot with deep stacks — lots of fold equity to exploit. A small, standard open to the min (200 total) maximizes my chance to pick up the blinds for free while keeping my risk limited. If either blind 3-bets I’ll fold this hand; if called I’ll play postflop in position, c-betting or pot-controlling depending on the board. Raising small here is a low-cost, high-reward steal attempt.

Pocket Aces is the strongest starting hand in Hold'em. Minni's button raise to 200 is likely wide given position and history of aggression. As SB, I want to 3-bet for value, isolate against worse hands, and build the pot while denying equity to Flash in BB. Raising to 700 (3.5x the raise) is a balanced size: gets value from pairs, suited connectors, broadways that Minni raises from button, while pressuring Flash's shorter stack. Folding or calling is suboptimal with AA; flat calling invites multiway pots.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Nine offsuit. Minni opened for 2x from the Button, and Elon has 3-bet from the Small Blind to 700. While King-Nine is a decent hand in some blind-defense scenarios, facing a 3-bet from the Small Blind while out of position against one or potentially two players is unfavorable. Elon has shown a lot of aggression recently and Minni is the chip leader. Calling 600 more into a 1000 pot would commit a significant portion of my stack (about 10%) with a hand that is often dominated by Elon's 3-betting range (AK, AQ, AJ, KQ, or pocket pairs). I will fold and wait for a better spot to commit chips.

I opened to 200 on the button and Elon (SB) 3-bet to 700 (Flash folded). I hold 3♠2♦ — one of the weakest hands. My plan was to fold to a 3-bet: calling 500 to see a flop (only ~5BB) is tempting because of position, but this hand has very poor equity and postflop playability against a likely strong 3-bet range. 4-betting as a bluff is unjustified (no meaningful blockers and would risk too much). With deep stacks and plenty of hands left, preserving my 20,050 stack for better spots and avoiding marginal, dominated situations is the highest EV line. I fold.

Preflop on the button with T6o (offsuit gapper but decent playability). SB Flash folded (pot includes dead money), BB Minni checked (limped, wide range). Deep stacks (mine 136BB effective). Button vs BB limp is a profitable spot to raise wide for steal/isolation/thin value. T6o has straight potential, blocks broadways somewhat, plays well multiway or heads-up in position. Raising small builds pot or takes it down. Size to 3x BB (300 total) standard and balanced. Folding too passive, calling poor pot odds/multiway risk.

Holding Q5o in the small blind against a button open from Elon is a clear fold. Q5o is a weak hand that plays poorly post-flop, especially out of position. Given that I am the shortest stack at the table, I should wait for higher equity hands to commit my chips.

Fold. 2♣8♣ is a very weak, low suited hand with poor playability out of position. Elon’s button open to 300 gives me only 200 more to call into a 450 pot (pot becomes 650) so I need ~30.8% equity to justify a call. Versus a typical BTN opening range my realistic equity with 2♣8♣ is well below that (hand is frequently dominated and has limited straight/flush potential), and I don’t want to play OOP multi-street with such a hand. Preserve my deep stack for better spots and defend with stronger/connected hands — fold and save the extra 200 chips.

On the button in a 3-handed game, Ace-Jack Offsuit (Ac Jh) is a very strong starting hand. My stack is 6300 chips (63 BB), and both opponents have larger stacks. A standard open-raise is effective here to build the pot and take control of the hand. Based on the previous hands, the standard opening size has been 300 chips, so I will raise to 300 to put pressure on the blinds and potentially win the pot pre-flop or go to the flop with a range advantage.

I hold 5d2d in the small blind. Flash opened to 300; the pot is 450 and I must call 250 (min raise to 500). 5-2 suited is one of the weakest hands — very little equity and poor playability out of position in a 3-way pot. Calling would put chips in with a hand that struggles postflop and facing action from the big blind as well; 3-betting is nonsense with this holding. Preserve my deep stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

3-handed, in BB facing button raise to 300 (3x). Flash has shorter stack of 6000. My 6d4d is suited connectors with straight/flush potential, decent speculative hand. Button range is wide (~40-50% hands), my equity is sufficient (~38-42% vs loose button open). Pot odds excellent: calling 200 into 450 (2.25:1 immediate). OOP postflop but dead money from blinds sweetens it. Raising not ideal as hand plays better multiway or vs missed flop. Calling to realize equity and control pot size.

Post-flop in BB OOP heads-up vs button raiser with 4d6d on QsTc3h board. Hand completely missed: no pair, weak gutshot straight draw to 5 for 3-4-5-6-7 (only 4 outs), backdoor flush draw. Board is connected and favors villain's wide button range (Qx, Tx, broadways, pairs). Pot 650, my stack 13450 effective vs Flash's 6000 shorter stack. No showdown value, poor playability OOP. Betting would be a bluff into a calling range, but too thin with reverse position. Check to control pot, induce bluffs, and fold to aggression while preserving stack. Realizing preflop equity was speculative; now folding equity post-flop.

I am the pre-flop aggressor on a board of Q-T-3 rainbow. While this board connects with many of the hands Elon might defend from the Big Blind, it also favors my range as the Button raiser. I hold Ace-high with a gutshot straight draw (King) and overcards. Since Elon checked to me, I will place a standard continuation bet of approximately half the pot. This exerts pressure on his marginal holdings and allows me to take down the pot immediately if he missed, while retaining equity to improve on later streets.

Facing a flop bet of 325 into 975 (pot odds ~3:1, requiring ~25% equity) OOP with 4d6d on QsTc3h. Hand is a complete airball: no pair, no strong draw—only a weak gutshot to the 5 (4 outs, ~9% equity) plus negligible backdoor flush draw. Board is coordinated (straight draws like JT, KJ, QJ, 89 possible), favoring villain's wide button continuation range (Qx, Tx, broadways, pocket pairs, Ax). Minimal showdown value and poor playability OOP vs aggressive shorter stack (Flash 5675). Raising as a bluff is thin: commits too much relative to stacks (~10% pot raise to 650 min, but sizing larger needed), reverse position hurts, and villain likely continues with decent made hands/draws. Calling realizes minimal equity while bloating pot OOP, setting up tough river spots. Discipline: preflop speculative call realized nothing postflop—fold to preserve stack for better spots in 3-handed play. Long-term EV positive by avoiding marginal situations.

Three-handed, I'm on the button with J♠4♠ (J4s). No one has raised yet — pot is 150 (SB+BB) and it costs 100 to just call. J4s is a playable, suited hand in position and I want to take the initiative and apply pressure to the blinds. I’ll open to 300 (the standard table sizing I’ve seen) to give myself fold equity and play the postflop pot in position. Plan: if both fold I scoop the blinds; if called I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops, control pot size on heavy A/K boards, and pursue backdoor flush/straight equity. If I face a significant 3-bet (large reraise) I’ll fold this dominated speculative hand rather than commit deep stacks.

K3o is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity against Minni's button opening range, which is wide but still dominates this hand often (e.g., better kings, any pair). Facing a raise to 300 from the button, calling 250 more from the small blind OOP commits me to a pot with a trash hand, leading to difficult postflop decisions where I'm crushed in most scenarios. Raising is not viable without a strong hand. Folding preserves my stack (13,400, healthy) for better spots in this tournament phase, avoiding spew. Minni raises frequently from history, but discipline is key to maximizing stack over remaining 40 hands.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Eight offsuit. Minni, on the button, has raised to 300. K8o is a standard defending hand against a button raise in a three-handed game. My stack is 65.5 big blinds, and I am facing a bet that requires me to call 200 to win a total pot of 450 (giving me 2.25:1 odds). Given the wide opening range of a button in short-handed play, K8o has enough equity and playability to see a flop. Raising is an option but might invite a 4-bet from a larger stack, so calling to see a flop is the most prudent and standard defensive play.

I am the Big Blind defending against a Button raise. On a Ten-high board with connected low cards (7, 6), I have not improved my King-Eight offsuit. The standard play here is to check to the pre-flop aggressor. Leading into the button on this board doesn't represent much strength, so I will check and evaluate based on Minni's continuation bet sizing.

I opened from the button and was called; Flash checks the flop. Board T♠-7♦-6♣ is somewhat coordinated and does hit parts of his calling range (pairs, T9/98, suited connectors), but he also has many missed overcards and random calls from the BB. I hold J♠4♠ — poor showdown value but a backdoor spade flush and the initiative as preflop aggressor. I’ll make a modest c-bet (~half the pot) to apply pressure, fold out pure overcards and unpaired hands, and keep the pot manageable if called. If he folds I scoop; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and only continue aggressively on cards that improve my equity (spade, J, or a very blank card); I’ll fold to strong raises since this hand can’t stand a big raise.

On a board of Ts-7d-6c, I have flopped a gutshot straight draw (needing a 9 to complete a 6-7-8-9-T straight). Minni has made a continuation bet of 300 into a pot of 650, which is less than half-pot. Given the pot odds (300 to win 1250 total) and the fact that I have one overcard (the King) that could potentially be live if I hit it, calling a small bet to see the turn is a reasonable play. My hand has enough equity against a wide button opening range to continue for at least one street.
I have a gutshot straight draw and one overcard on a paired board. Checking to the pre-flop aggressor is the standard line here. I'll see how Minni acts before deciding whether to continue.

I'm on the button with J♠4♠. Board: T♠ 7♦ 6♣ 7♣. I c-bet the flop and was called; villain checked the turn. The turn pairs the board and kills my backdoor spade plan (I needed both turn+river spades on the flop). I now only have 6 outs (3 Js + 3 4s) ≈ 13% to improve by the river, so my raw equity is low. Flash has shown a tendency to call flop c-bets and to continue with a wide range that includes Tx, 7x, 6x and various connectors — meaning my fold equity on a turn bluff is limited and a bet will often be called by better hands. Pot control is important here: betting risks building a pot I have little equity in and opens me to a raise I should fold to. I will check to keep the pot manageable, see the river, and re-evaluate: if villain bets a small amount on the river when I improve (J or 4) I can call/raise; if he bets large on the turn I will muck; if he checks back we go to river with a plan to bluff only with a very convincing blank (or value-bet if I pair).

The river has brought a third seven, making the board Ts-7d-6c-7c-7h. My hand is currently Trip 7s with a Ten and a King kicker (7-7-7-T-K). After Minni checked back on the turn, it's possible my King-high has some showdown value against missed draws or lower high cards (like QJ or J9). However, I am still beaten by any Ace, any Ten, any pocket pair, or of course, the case seven. Betting wouldn't likely fold out hands that beat me (like a Ten or an Ace) and wouldn't be called by hands I beat. Therefore, checking to see a cheap showdown or evaluate a bet is the most logical step.

Board: T♠-7♦-6♣-7♣-7♥. I hold J♠4♠ — no pair, no flush, no straight. I was the aggressor preflop and on the flop but the turn and river were both checked by Flash. The river now pairs the board into trips (three 7s), which is a very scary board for a hand with no showdown value. Flash called my flop c‑bet, so his calling range contains a lot of pairs and some strong made hands (Tx, 7x, 6x, some straights/connected hands) and he’s shown a willingness to simply check‑control the later streets rather than barrel; that makes bluffing him here unlikely to work very often. I also don’t block many of his value combos (I don’t hold a 7, 10 or 6), so my fold equity is low. Betting would risk building a pot I have virtually no equity in for only a small chance to fold out marginal overcards — not worth it. Best play is pot control: check and fold to any meaningful bet. If Flash checks back we get the pot for free (or can show, but likely muck). Action: check.

AQo is a premium hand from the button in a 3-handed pot. With no action before me (just blinds posted), this is a standard spot to open-raise for value and to thin the field or take initiative. Raising to 3x the BB (300) is aggressive but balanced, given effective stacks are deep (mine 134BB, opponents covered). Position allows control post-flop. Folding or limping is too passive; larger raise risks too much without reads.

I am in the Small Blind with Queen-Six offsuit. Elon, on the Button, has raised to 300. This is a very weak hand to play out of position against an aggressive button raise, especially with a deep-stacked player like Minni still to act in the Big Blind. The best strategic move here is to conserve my stack and fold.

Elon opened to 300 from the button and SB folded; I'm in the big blind with K♥3♠ (100 already posted). I will call (300 total) — heads-up I should defend a wide range and Kx has reasonable showdown equity and blockers. 3-betting K3o is thin and often dominated, and I prefer to keep the pot manageable out of position with a marginal hand. Plan: evaluate the flop — continue aggressively if I pair the King or pick up strong equity, otherwise control the pot and fold to heavy pressure.

I called preflop with K♥3♠ to defend a wide BB range heads-up. The flop 4♦-4♣-J♥ is very dry and relatively safe for checking — my King is only a high card with no real draws. Betting OOP with a marginal hand just to fold out bluffs is unnecessary; checking controls the pot and lets me call a reasonable c‑bet to realize equity while folding to heavy aggression. Plan: check here; call small-to-medium continuation bets (≈<=1/2 pot) and fold to large raises, re-evaluating on the turn if I hit a King or the board changes significantly.

AQ completely misses this paired, dry flop (4d4cJh), but as the preflop raiser from the button in position against a BB caller who checked, a standard continuation bet is optimal. The board is low and paired, which polarizes her checking range towards weak hands or traps, but my wide button opening range (including AJ, overpairs, broadways) hits this flop reasonably well. Betting ~60% pot (400) applies pressure, takes down the pot often from missed draws/weak aces, gets value from worse hands like AJ/JT/Ax, and denies equity to her floaters. Position allows me to control if she calls/raises. Folding or checking is too passive with initiative and stack depth (131BB). Larger bet risks getting raised off equity unnecessarily.

Call 400. Heads-up in the big blind I should defend a wide range and Elon’s flop bet (400 into 1050 ≈ 0.38 pot) is a small continuation bet I can comfortably call with marginal Kx. K♥3♠ has some showdown value and blocks KJ/Kx combos; the board is dry (4♦-4♣-J♥) so raising OOP with this hand is thin and often dominated. I’ll call to keep the pot manageable, realize equity, and re-evaluate on the turn — folding to significant aggression unless I pair my King or pick up strong additional equity.

Board is double‑paired (4♦ 4♣ J♥ J♦). With K♥3♠ I have no pair — my best play is pot control. Elon (button) opened preflop and made a small flop c‑bet, then checked the turn; that line favors missed broadway hands or medium strength hands more than a slowplayed J/4, but both are possible. Betting OOP with only K3 gives me little fold equity and risks getting raised or committed by actual J/4 value hands or by hands that call and beat my kicker at showdown. I’ll check to keep the pot small, see the river, and then decide: call small river bets (≈≤½ pot) to realize equity versus missed broadways and fold to large river bets that strongly represent a full house.

The turn pairs the jack and completes a diamond flush draw on this already paired board (44JJ). My AQo is pure ace-high bluff catcher with no pair, straight draw, or flush potential. Minni's flop check-call indicates medium strength like Jx, slowplayed sets, or stubborn Ax/draws, which likely have me beat or dominated. A bet here polarizes my range too thin, gets raised by better (JJ,44,J4), called by similar, and folds out weaker hands I beat. With deep stacks (me ~88BB effective vs her 128BB) and position, checking behind is optimal pot control: induces bluffs on scary river, allows cheap showdown if she checks through, or free card. No value to bet, equity realization better by checking.

Board: 4♦ 4♣ J♥ J♦ 2♥. I hold K♥3♠ — no pair, just King-high. Opponent (button) opened preflop, made a small flop c‑bet and then checked the turn; that line can include lots of Jx/4x combos (which are full houses now) plus medium-strength broadway hands that might check the turn. On this heavily paired board my hand has essentially no showdown value and very poor fold equity as a bluff vs a competent opener — many hands that beat me will not fold to a river bet, and betting risks getting raised by a monster. With a 1450 pot and healthy stacks, the lowest-variance, highest-expected-value line is pot control: check and take showdown. If Elon fires a small river bet I’ll consider a call only if his sizing and timing look like a bluff (≈≤½ pot); fold to a large river bet representing value.

River board is 4d4cJhJd2h, pairing the board heavily with full house possibilities (JJ/44/J4) and completing minor draws. AQo is ace-high bluff-catcher with no improvement. Preflop: raised button 300, Minni BB called (wide defender). Flop check-call of 400 into paired 44Jh suggests Jx (AJs, KJs, etc.), Ax draw, or sets/traps. Turn Jd pairs Js (completes diamonds); her check, my check preserves thin equity. River 2h blank for most ranges. Her check again indicates caution (fear of full house, or weak Jx wanting showdown). Betting risks value from worse (bluffs/Ax unlikely after flop call) while getting called/raised by better (any J, 44). Checking behind maximizes EV: free showdown vs her possible mid-Jx/Ax, induces if she had nothing, pot control with deep stacks (127BB effective). No fold equity worth risking vs passive line. Standard check.

I am on the button with Js7d, which is a marginal hand even in a three-handed game. My stack is 7,250 (72.5 BB), making me the shortest stack at the table. Both Minni and Elon have been playing aggressively and frequently defending their blinds or 3-betting pre-flop. Given the stack dynamics and the low value of J7o, there's little reason to risk chips on a steal attempt that is likely to be contested. I will fold and wait for a stronger starting hand.

Pocket Aces — this is the best possible starting hand and I must raise rather than limp or just call. Pot is 150, to-call 50 and min-raise is 200; I’ll open to ~3x the big blind (raise to 300). That size builds a decent pot with deep stacks (I have 18.55k vs Elon 14.05k), keeps a wide calling range on his part, and gives me flexibility postflop. Plan: if Elon folds we pickup the 150; if he calls we proceed OOP with the best hand and extract value on favorable runouts; if he 3-bets large I’ll evaluate sizing and likely 4-bet for value or flatten to play for stacks depending on his sizing.

Preflop in BB with 5d2d, a weak suited connector. Minni raised to 300 from SB after Flash folded button, indicating a potential steal but still a strong enough range to fold trash like this OOP. Pot odds are decent (200 to call into ~600), but equity is low (~30-40% vs wide open range) and playability is terrible out of position with low cards that rarely make strong hands. Minni is aggressive historically but folding preserves stack for better spots in deepstack tournament. Standard fold.

I'm on the button with A♠2♠ — a strong suited ace and an ideal hand to open from position. The pot is just the blinds (150) and calling 100 would be passive; I want to apply pressure to the blinds and isolate opponents. A standard open to 300 (≈3×BB in this game, and only a small portion of my 18,700 stack) should pick up the blinds often and gives me position if called. Plan: fold to a large, polarized 3-bet from Elon; against a single call I will continuation-bet dry boards and play carefully on wetter boards, using position and my nut-flush potential.

T5o is a trash hand with poor playability out of position in the SB. Minni's button raise is likely wide, but calling 250 OOP into a pot of 450 gives poor implied odds and sets up difficult postflop spots against an aggressive opponent. Flash still to act and could 3-bet. Folding preserves stack for better spots.

I am in the Big Blind with Jack-Four offsuit. Minni, the chip leader, has raised to 300 from the button, and Elon has folded. Although I am getting decent pot odds to call (200 to win a total pot of 650), J4o is a very weak hand to play out of position against a loose-aggressive chip leader. I have around 71 big blinds, which gives me enough room to wait for a more favorable hand rather than defending with junk and potentially getting into a difficult post-flop situation.

3-handed on the button with 64h suited connectors, a highly playable hand for steals and post-flop potential due to straight/flush draws and position. Blinds post 150 total, pot odds favorable for a raise. Standard open-raise size of 3x BB (300) to build pot, deny equity, and take initiative. My stack is 14k effective vs SB 7.1k, BB 18.75k, deep enough for post-flop play. Opponents show aggression but fold to button raises often in history.

I am in the Small Blind with Q3o, a weak hand. Elon has raised from the Button to 300. In a three-handed game, Button ranges are typically wide, but Q3o is not a strong enough hand to defend against a standard raise, especially out of position. Minni is still to act in the Big Blind and has shown aggression in previous hands. Folding is the most prudent play to preserve my stack for better opportunities as we approach the end of the tournament.

Elon (button) opened to 300, Flash folded. I'm in the big blind with 3♣5♠. The pot is 450 and I need to add 200 to call (total 300), which gives me ~30.8% equity needed — favorable pot odds. Heads-up the button's opening range is wide, so defending from the big blind is standard. 3-5 offsuit is weak for a preflop 3-bet/bluff (min-raising to 500 would put me out of position and create difficult postflop decisions with a marginal hand). I'll call to see a flop and proceed cautiously: continue with pair/strong draws, otherwise give up to heavy aggression OOP.

I hold 3♣5♠ on Ac 7c 2s — no pair and no meaningful draw (only a very unlikely two-card club runner or a runner-runner wheel). My preflop plan was to continue only with a pair or strong draws; this flop meets neither. Out of position vs the button, it’s poor to fire or turn this into a bluff-heavy spot. I’ll check and plan to fold to any sizable continuation bet, only reluctantly calling very small C-bets to probe.

Preflop button raise with 64hh got one caller from BB, a frequent caller but folds to postflop aggression often based on history. Flop Ac7c2s is a good c-bet spot: board is Ace-high with a club draw, favoring my button raising range which includes many Ax, sets (22,77,AA), and strong clubs. My hand has backdoor flush and straight potential, plus fold equity vs Minni's checking range (weak pairs, draws, air). Position allows control if called. Bet 2/3 pot (~400) to charge draws, get value from worse Ax/77/22, and deny equity. Pot 650, SPR deep enough post-bet.

Board: Ac 7c 2s. I have 3c5s — no pair and only a very remote backdoor club flush (need both turn+river clubs). Pot is 1050 and it costs 400 to call, so I need ~400 / (1050+400) ≈ 27.6% equity. My realistic equity (about 6 outs to pair by river ≈ 24% plus a small runner-runner flush chance) is marginal at best, and many of those hits still lose to an Ace or better from a button c-betting range. I’m out of position, have little fold equity, and calling or raising will create difficult postflop decisions with a weak holding. Stick to the preflop plan to give up to substantial C-bets — fold and wait for a better spot.

I am on the button with Qd-4c, a relatively weak holding. While the button offers a positional advantage, a Queen with a low kicker like 4 is easily dominated. Looking at the stack sizes, I have 71 BB (7,100 chips) while both opponents have significantly larger stacks (185 BB and 142.5 BB). In this 3-handed situation, while widening the opening range is common, Qd-4c is at the bottom of the range and doesn't play well against a potential 3-bet or if I get called. Folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

Heads-up to the big blind with QcTh in the small blind. Pot is 150 and I only need 50 to call, but Q-T is a strong, playable hand heads-up with good broadway and straight potential. Standard table open size is ~300 and with deep stacks I want to seize the initiative, isolate Elon in the BB, deny him good odds to limp/call, and build a pot with a hand that plays well postflop. I’ll raise to 300 to take initiative; if Elon 3-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on size and his tendencies (tight 3-bet = fold, light/small 3-bet = call or 4-bet depending on sizing).

A♠T♠ is a premium suited ace hand with excellent playability, blockers to AA/AK, and flush/straight potential. Facing a small raise to 300 from Minni in the SB after BTN folds, her range is wide and includes many weaker aces, broadways, suited hands, and bluffs. From hand history, Minni is aggressive with frequent small raises but folds to 3-bets and post-flop pressure often (e.g., Hands 1-114 show her folding to raises multiple times). Defending BB wide here, but 3-betting polarizes our range, gets value from worse, fold equity from junk, and builds pot OOP with strong equity (~45-55% vs her open range). Calling risks multiway if Flash wakes up (unlikely), and flat allows floats. Raising to 900 (3x her raise) is standard size: pot-committing lightly (800 more into 400+800=1200 pot effective), with deep stacks allowing post-flop flexibility. Fold is too tight for these odds and hand strength.

I opened to 300 from the SB with QcTh and Elon 3-bet to 900 (pot 1200). I only need to commit 600 more to call and I’ll have position postflop (button/small blind acts last after the flop), which is a big edge. QTo offsuit is very playable heads-up — good showdown equity vs. a wide 3-bet range and good connectivity to realize on many flops. A 4-bet to the minimum (1500) is too small to generate reliable fold equity and just bloats the pot with a marginal hand; folding is too passive given our hand and position. With deep stacks I prefer to call, see a flop, and use position to control the pot or apply pressure based on the texture. Call to 900.

Board is 4s 9h Jd and I hold Qc Th. This is a marginal but playable hand heads‑up: I have two overcards and a double‑gutshot to the straight (Ks or 8s) plus backdoor equity. Versus Elon’s heads‑up 3‑bet range I have reasonable equity (~30–40%), and I have position. I don’t want to bloatedly commit with just queen‑high and a draw — I prefer to use position to realize equity and to induce bluffs. Plan: check and evaluate Elon’s c‑bet. If he fires a small/medium c‑bet (typical 1/3–2/3 pot) I’ll call and re‑evaluate on the turn; I’ll fold to an overbearingly large shove/raise that prices out my draws. If he checks back I’ll take the initiative on many favorable turns. Action is pot control and realization of equity rather than building the pot out of position.

Flop: 4s9hJd. Heads-up vs Minni (SB) who checked. My AsTs has the nut flush draw (9 outs, ~35% equity), ace-high blocker, and a gutshot straight draw to Qd/Qs/broadway (~4 more outs). Strong semi-bluff spot with 40-50% equity vs her 3-bet calling range (pairs like 88-JJ, suited broadway, Ax suited, Jx, 98s, etc.). Board is coordinated with flush draw and gutshots, hitting some of her value but also draws she might float. She checks first (weakness or trap, but history shows Minni passive post-flop when checked to, folding to c-bets often e.g., Hands 24,30,40). Betting denies her equity (e.g., lower flush draws, OESD), gets value from Jx/9x/weak pairs, fold equity from air/floats. Pot 1800, bet 2/3 pot (1200) balances value/semi-bluff, keeps pot manageable OOP (acting 2nd post-flop), sets up turns well. Checking risks giving free cards to overcards/draws or induces bluffs but lower EV with this draw. Minni's stack deep (17k), mine 13k, but this bet ~9% stack low risk/high reward.

Elon c-bet 1200 into a ~3000 pot (~28.6% break-even equity to call). QcTh has decent heads‑up equity: one overcard (Q), a double‑gutshot (K or 8) plus Q pairing outs — roughly 11 meaningful outs and comfortably better equity than the break‑even threshold versus a wide 3‑bet/c‑bet range. I have position and deep stacks, so I want to realize equity and keep the pot controllable rather than bloating it with a marginal hand. Raising here would commit too much without a made hand; folding is too passive. Plan: call 1200, see the turn, and on the turn value/raise if I pair or make the straight, or use position to float/bluff or fold to extreme aggression if I miss and Elon barrels strongly.

Elon bet 1,200 into a 4,200 pot — I only need ~22% equity to justify a call. With QcTh on 4s-9h-Jd-2s I have an open‑ended straight draw to K/8 (8 outs) plus 3 queens and 3 tens (≈14 outs total, ~30% to improve on the river), and I have position. Given deep stacks and Elon's line (preflop 3‑bet and smallish turn stab), calling preserves pot control, realizes equity, and keeps bluffing/river options open. A raise here is too thin and can get me committed with just a draw; folding is overly tight. Plan: call the 1,200, see the river — if I pair or complete the straight I’ll value bet; if I miss I’ll evaluate a delayed bluff only on favorable river cards or check/fold to heavy action.

On the turn 2s, the board is 4s-9h-Jd-2s. My AsTs gives me the nut spade flush draw (9 outs) and a gutshot straight draw to Q (4 outs, some overlap), for massive equity (~35-40% vs. villain's range). Two spades on board hit some of Minni's possible spade draws, but my A blocker to the nut flush is huge, and she likely doesn't have it often. She checked flop (weakness), called my c-bet 1200 into ~2700 pot (pot odds favorable for her calls/draws), and checks turn again, indicating possible medium strength (Jx/9x, underpairs, weaker draws) or trapping monsters (unlikely given history). From 114 hands, Minni often calls pre/flop then folds to continued aggression (e.g., Hands 5,20,24,33,38, etc.), especially OOP after check-calls. Pot 4200, my stack 12250 effective (~13bb-ish? Wait, blinds 50/100 but deep effective), bet ~2/3 pot (2800) builds pot for river shove if check-called, gets value from Jx/9x/pairs that beat bluffs but lose to flush, fold equity from floats/missed draws/A-high, denies equity to her remaining draws (e.g., heart flush draws, lower spade draws). Checking risks free river card to her OESD/backdoors or induces bluffs but lower EV with nuts draw OOP vs passive line. Semi-bluff maximizes EV long-term stack growth in late tournament.

Elon’s line (preflop 3‑bet, small flop stab called, now a larger turn bet) is aggressive but wide enough that I still have meaningful equity. Calling 2,800 into a 7,000 pot requires ~28.6% equity. With QcTh on 4s‑9h‑Jd‑2s I have 8 straight outs (K/8) plus 3 queens and 3 tens ≈ 14 outs (~30% to hit the river) — just above the break‑even threshold. I also have position, which lets me see the river and either extract value if I hit or take a well‑timed bluff/controlled fold if I miss and he barrels heavy. Raising here (min to 5,600) is too thin — it bloats the pot with a draw and polarizes me into uncomfortable spots versus a likely calling range. Folding would be overly tight given the pot odds and my equity. Plan: call 2,800, see the river; if I complete (pair or straight) I will bet for value; if I miss I will evaluate a delayed bluff only on favourable rivers or check‑fold to strong continued aggression.

I missed — QcTh on 4s‑9h‑Jd‑2s‑9d has no pair and no completed straight. Elon opened, 3‑bet pre, bet flop and turned then checked the river; that line is consistent with both medium strength (Jx, some pairs) and missed bluffs/draws. Pot = 9,800 and there is no bet to call, so a small blocking/bluff bet can win the pot outright while keeping my risk controlled. A 2,000 bet requires ~17% fold equity to be breakeven (2,000 / (9,800+2,000) ≈ 17%), which is achievable here given Elon checked the river and will fold some proportion of missed/weak hands. Plan: make a modest bluff to fold out hands like A/K high, some KQ/AQ, and seize the pot; if he calls I assume he has a better hand and I’ll let it go if he raises. This balances risk (I only risk 2k for a 9.8k pot) with upside (winning the whole pot now).

River 9d pairs the 9s on a 4s-9h-Jd-2s-9d board. My AsTs missed the nut spade flush draw completely (still just four spades total) and has no pair, straight, or other draw -- pure ace-high with a Ts kicker. Equity is dismal against Minni's check-call-check-call-bet range after two barrels from me, which she defended twice OOP. Her range is heavily value-oriented: trips with any 9x (A9s, K9s, T9s, 98s, etc., ~15-20% of her calling range), top pair Jx (AJs, KJs, QJs, ~20%), two pair J9 (~5%), or better (sets/JJ rare but possible). Overpairs QQ+ would struggle to check-call flop/turn and now bet into paired board. Bluff frequency low here -- she floated or defended draws pre/flop but called two sizable bets (1200 + 2800 = 4000 total), indicating showdown value; pure air rarely survives double barrel on J-high coordinated board. Her small river bet (2000 into 11800, ~17% pot) is thin value/inducement from medium strength, not a bluff polaron. Pot odds ~6.9:1 superficially good (need ~13% equity), but realized equity <10% vs polarized-but-value-skewed range (showdown dominated + reverse implied odds if I call turn trips). Raising accomplishes nothing (she snaps with better or folds worse I beat). Calling risks spew OOP in 3-handed late tourney (hand 115/150), bloating pot with air when stacks are ~90bb effective but goal is stack maximization -- folding preserves 9450 to play premium spots. History confirms Minni capable of trapping value (e.g., Hand 29 called down river, Hand 56 called big bets) but folds aggression often; here her line screams value. Hero-fold maximizes EV long-term.

I’m on the button in a 3-handed pot with deep stacks (I have ~234 BB). T♥5♦ is marginal, but in position I should open very wide to apply pressure and steal the blinds. The table’s standard open sizing is ~300, and sizing to 300 gives me meaningful fold equity versus the SB/BB while keeping postflop playability. Plan: if I’m called I’ll use position to C-bet favorable flops and fold to strong resistance; if faced with a sizeable 3‑bet I’ll let this marginal hand go. Raising also avoids letting the blinds see a cheap flop for free.

Qh3s is a trash hand offsuit with no connectivity or suited potential. Facing a button raise from aggressive Minni, from SB position, this is a clear fold. Poor equity vs wide button range, bad post-flop playability, and no need to defend wide with deep stacks. Preserve chips for premium spots to maximize stack long-term.

I am in the Big Blind holding King-high (Kd 5h). The button (Minni) has raised to 3x the big blind. In 3-handed play, King-high is a reasonable hand to defend against a button open, especially given the pot odds (calling 200 to win a pot that will be 650). While I will be out of position, folding a King to a standard button raise is too tight. I will call and see a flop, looking to hit a King or favorable board texture.

I have bottom pair with a King kicker on a Queen-high board. After calling from the Big Blind pre-flop, the standard procedure is to check to the initial aggressor (Minni) to see how they interact with the board. Leading out into the button raiser with bottom pair would be overplaying the hand. I will check and evaluate based on the size of a potential continuation bet.

I have T5o and just paired the 5 on Qs‑8d‑5s — a middle pair with a 10 kicker in position. Flash (BB) checked, and his preflop calling range is wide and fairly sticky, so a small/medium c‑bet will accomplish value and protection: it extracts from worse pairs, weak Ax/Kx, and some draws while denying free cards to overcards. Pot is 650, so a bet around half (I choose 300) gives good fold equity and a price that sticky opponents will often call when behind. If I get called I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn; if I face a substantial raise I’ll usually fold this vulnerable middle pair. Bet 300 to take the initiative and build/defend equity in position.

I have bottom pair (5s) with a King kicker. Minni's bet of 300 into a 650 pot is a standard continuation bet that can represent a wide range of hands, including many that I currently beat or have decent equity against. Given the pot odds (calling 300 to win a total pot of 1250), I only need about 24% equity to make this a profitable call. Bottom pair is sufficient to see a turn card, where I can improve to trips or two pair, or potentially reach a showdown if the board stays relatively safe.

On the turn, I have bottom pair (fives) with a King kicker. The Ten of Clubs is a somewhat coordinated card that completes some straight draws (like J9) and improves hands like QT or T8. As the out-of-position player who called pre-flop and on the flop, my standard play is to check to the initial aggressor (Minni). Leading here would not accomplish much, as better hands will call and worse hands will fold. I will check and see how Minni reacts to the turn card.

Turn card (Tc) paired my Ten, so I now hold two pair (Tens and Fives). Pot is 1,250 and Flash checked after calling my flop c‑bet, so his range is wide and fairly sticky (Qx, 8x, small pairs, some straight/connector hands and occasional bluffs). This is a clear value‑bet spot — many one‑pair hands and weaker holdings will call, and I want to deny free cards that could turn a set/straight on the river. I size to ~2/3 pot (800): big enough to extract value from top/second pair and charge most draws, but not so large that I bloat the pot unnecessarily versus a hand that can beat me (sets/straights). If Flash proceeds to a large check‑raise or shove (a line consistent with sets/turned straights), I’ll strongly reassess and will often fold to avoid getting stacked by a better made hand; small raises I’ll evaluate and can call. Bet 800 to keep initiative and maximize value from worse hands.

On the turn, the board has become significantly more coordinated with the addition of the Ten of Clubs. This card completes straight draws (J9) and improves many of the hands in a button-opening range (QT, T8, etc.). My bottom pair of fives with a King kicker is now extremely vulnerable, and Minni is showing continued aggression with a 2/3 pot bet of 800. While I have a decent stack, calling here puts me in a difficult position on the river against a chip-leading opponent who is likely to fire a third bullet. Given the board texture and the strength shown by the opponent, bottom pair is no longer a viable hand to continue with. I will fold and preserve my 65 BB stack for a more favorable situation.

3-handed on the button with K4o pre-flop, first to act. Excellent position against blinds. K4o is playable with king blocker and decent high card value in short-handed play where ranges are wide. Limping is suboptimal; folding too tight given position and stack depths (94BB effective vs SB, deeper vs BB). Standard raise size of 3x BB to 300 builds pot, applies pressure, and takes advantage of wide blind defending ranges seen in history (frequent calls/limps). History shows aggressive play from Minni but from position we can exploit post-flop.

I am in the Small Blind with King-Four offsuit. Elon, the Button, has raised to 3x (300). My hand is relatively weak and does not play well out of position, especially against a player who has shown aggression in previous hands. Given that Minni is in the Big Blind with a massive stack, I risk getting squeezed or playing a multi-way pot out of position with a mediocre king. Folding is the most disciplined and safe play here to preserve my stack.

Elon opened to 300 from the button (3x). With Qc8c in the big blind I have a playable, suited queen with good postflop playability and blocking value. Pot is 450 and I only need to call 200 more (300 total) — the price and implied odds justify defending. A tiny raise to 500 as the minimum 3-bet has limited fold equity and would put me out of position facing a wider 4-bet/stacked pot; I prefer to realize equity and play postflop deep. Plan: call to 300, evaluate the flop — continue aggressively on club-heavy or queen/8 flops, check-call reasonable c-bets with draws or medium pairs, and fold to heavy turn/river pressure on dry, unfavorable textures.

Check. I have Qc8c on 5s-6s-Ac: only a weak hand with a two-card backdoor club draw and a queen blocker, out of position vs the preflop raiser. No value to lead here and I don’t want to bloat the pot OOP with just backdoor equity. Checking controls the pot and lets Elon c-bet so I can decide — I’ll call small-to-medium continuation bets to realize equity (and because the Ac+Qc interaction gives some blocker value), but fold to heavy pressure on a dry turn/river. If Elon checks back I can take a free card or probe-bet a club/queen turn. Stack depths (I: 23,800; Elon: 9,100) give me room to apply pressure later, so pot control now is the prudent line.

Flop of 5s 6s Ac is draw-heavy with flush and straight draws possible. Minni's check from BB after calling pre-flop suggests a wide range including Ax, pairs, draws, or floats. As pre-flop raiser on button with position and deep stacks (94BB effective vs Minni), a continuation bet is standard to deny equity to draws, protect king-high, and exploit her checking range. No showdown value, so betting takes it down often or builds pot when ahead. Half-pot size of 400 into 650 balances range, applies pressure without overcommitting. History shows Minni calls flops but folds to aggression later; position allows control post-flop.

Call 400. Qc8c on 5s-6s-Ac is not a made hand, but it has real equity (6 outs to pair the Q or 8 and backdoor club possibilities). Pot is 1,050; calling 400 makes the effective pot 1,450, so my break-even equity is ~400/1,450 ≈ 27.6%. My rundown: ~24% to pair by the river (6 outs) plus ~4% to complete a two-card backdoor club flush, so my equity vs a reasonably wide c-bet range is right around the required threshold — a marginal but +EV call. Folding would be too nitty given the price; raising OOP with nothing but backdoor equity is too thin (limited fold equity, and I don’t want to bloat a pot out of position against a preflop raiser). Plan after the call: reevaluate on the turn — continue (call or even raise) if I improve (pair or club-runner), or fold to heavy turn/river pressure on dry bricks. If Elon checks back I can take a free card or probe-bet a club/queen turn.

Check. Elon checked the turn (board 5s 6s Ac Js) and I hold Qc8c — I have no pair and the turn did not bring a club or a queen, so my only realistic outs are the Q or 8 (6 outs ≈ 13% to hit on the river). The two-card backdoor club possibility is now dead. The pot is 1,450; betting OOP into that with so little equity would be a thin bluff and likely to be called or raised by Ax and pocket pairs. With deep stacks and out of position, pot control and seeing a free river is the +EV line. Plan: check and evaluate the river — if I pair Q/8 I can value-bet, otherwise fold to heavy river aggression.

Turn board brings the Js, completing numerous straight draws (e.g., 7T, 78, T7) and adding another spade to the already draw-heavy 5s6sAc flop, making the board extremely coordinated and scary. My K4o is pure king-high with no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw (gutshot at best pre-turn but irrelevant now), and minimal blockers. Minni's check-call flop and check-turn suggest a wide range: weak Ax (KQ, KJ suited, etc.), small pairs, missed draws turning into floats, or monster traps like sets/AJ. However, her history shows frequent calling stations tendencies but folding to persistent aggression on scary boards (e.g., Hands 20, 24, 35). With position and ~6x pot (1450 pot, 8700 stack), checking behind realizes equity safely with king-high, controls the pot on a board where I'm likely behind medium strength, avoids bloating into her deep stack (23400), and keeps options open on river: value bet if bricks and reps strength, bluff if scare card or she checks weak. Firing a second barrel as a bluff has fold equity but risks her hero-calling with top pair/weak ace or flatting strong hands, turning my hand face-up. Deep effective stack (87BB) favors pot control over marginal bluff here; checking maximizes EV long-term in tournament setting with 33 hands left.

I checked the turn with Qc8c and planned to take a free river — the Td is a complete blank for my hand. I didn't pair and have virtually no showdown value (only queen-high plus a useless club blocker). Elon's line (preflop raise, flop c-bet, then check on the turn) is consistent with Ax, pocket pairs, J/T combos or some medium hands that are unlikely to fold to a pure river bluff very often. Betting OOP here as a bluff is thin given the pot (1,450) and his stack — I have little fold equity and bad blocker coverage of his strongest holdings. Pot-control is the best line: check and conserve chips; I will fold to any river bet and only win if he also checks with air.

River Td is a relatively blank card that doesn't complete the spade flush draw or add significant new straight possibilities beyond what was already threatened (board remains highly coordinated with A-high straights, two-pair combos, etc.). My Kh4c is still pure king-high no pair, no draw completed, but blocks some broadway hands like AKQJ. Minni's line: calling PF raise OOP wide range, check-call flop c-bet (missed or weak Ax/pairs/draws), check turn check back (pot control or weakness), check river (no value or giving up). History shows Minni calls flops/streets frequently (e.g., Hand 29, 60, 73) but folds rivers to aggression on scary boards when checked to (e.g., Hand 20, 35, 115). With 8700 stack (6BB pot ~0.17 of stack), position, and deep effective vs her 23400, a bluff bet (e.g., 1000 half-pot) has merit: high fold equity vs her capped range (weak Ax like A7-A9, small pairs 77-22/66-44? but 5-6 there, missed draws now bricked). Pot odds good if >60% fold equity, plausible given check-check turn signaling no medium strength. However, checking maximizes EV: realizes all equity with king-high (often best vs floats/small pairs), avoids hero-call from Ax/top pair (she calls wide), preserves stack for better spots with 33 hands left in tourney. Bluff risks turning hand face-up if called/raised, while check wins pot often uncontested. Pot control from turn continues; checking best.

I checked the turn planning to fold to river aggression with no improvement — the Td is a complete blank for Qc8c and I have only queen-high with no flush. Elon raised preflop, c‑bet the flop and then checked the turn; his river bet (1,000 into the 1,450 pot, making the pot 2,450) is a sizable ~69% pot-sized bet. Calling 1,000 would require ~29% equity (1,000 / 3,450) to break even, but my hand has essentially 0% showdown improvement left and only wins when he’s bluffing. His line is very consistent with many value hands that beat me (KQ made a Broadway straight, JT/TX two‑pair or sets, A‑x, etc.), and I block too few bluffs to justify a raise. Per my turn plan, folding to heavy river pressure is the +EV line.

I am on the button with Td6s, which is a marginal hand in a 3-handed game. The pot is currently 150 (small and big blind). While standard strategy often involves opening the button to steal blinds, I have a relatively short stack compared to Minni and Elon. T6o is a bit weak for a raise in this specific context, but folding is also a safe option to wait for a better spot given my stack size of 64.5 big blinds. However, at a 3-handed table, you have to be wider. Let's look at the current action. It's pre-flop and it's my turn to act first. Since I am first to act on the button, I will fold this weak holding and wait for a more premium hand to commit chips.

I have pocket Kings (Ks Kc) in the small blind, Flash folded so it’s heads-up vs Elon (BB 100, ~10050 stack). Pot is 150 and it only costs me 50 to see the flop, but K-K is a premium hand I want to build and isolate with rather than limp. Standard sizing and table history favor a 3xBB-style open; raising to 300 (I’ve already posted 50, so +250) extracts value from worse hands (Ax, broadways, medium pairs), discourages speculative limp/calling ranges, and keeps postflop maneuverability with deep stacks. If Elon 3-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing (small 3-bet → consider 4-betting/calling, very large 3-bet → fold or call depending on pot odds and reads), but preflop the +300 raise is the highest EV play.

Preflop in BB facing a 3x raise from SB after BTN fold. Pot 400, call 200 more (getting over 3:1). A2o has decent equity (~35-40%) vs a wide SB opening range, which Minni appears to play aggressively based on history (frequent raises). OOP postflop but excellent pot odds warrant defending wide, including weak Ax. Postflop plan: check-call cbet on favorable boards, check-raise strong draws or ace-high flops. Avoid 3-bet as it's not strong enough for value and fold equity uncertain against deep aggro Minni. Folding too tight here.

I hold KK on a dry flop (4c 7c 5s) heads-up vs Elon who just called preflop. My hand is a clear overpair to the board and only very narrow parts of his calling range (sets: 44/77/55) currently beat me. I want to extract value from worse pocket pairs, Ax/broadways and thin pairs while charging any backdoor/turn equity, and I don’t want to give free cards. I’ll c-bet about half the pot to accomplish that: bet 300 into the 600 pot. If he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and continue for value/protection; if he check-raises big (polarized) I’ll respect the range and re-evaluate folding or pot-control depending on sizing.

Facing a 1/3 pot cbet on a drawy flop (two clubs, open-enders possible) from aggressive SB raiser Minni. Pot 900, call 300 for ~4:1 odds. A2o holds Ace-high, blocks strong Ax hands, has ~30-40% equity vs her polarized SB cbet range (overpairs, sets, strong draws, bluffs). No strong draw myself, but good pot odds and position OOP warrant calling to realize equity and control pot on turn if check-through. Raising risks bloating pot OOP without made hand or strong draw; folding too tight given preflop defense and live range. Plan: call, evaluate turn (fold to big bet if missed, value bet/raise if improves). Deep stacks (9.8k eff vs her 22k) allow maneuvering.

I hold KK (K♠K♣) on a turn of 4♣ 7♣ 5♠ 9♣. This is still a very strong hand (an overpair) but the 9♣ completes a three-club board — the only hands that currently beat me are sets and made flushes (i.e. opponents who began with two clubs). Elon called my flop c-bet, which indicates a range that contains pocket pairs, broadways/Ax, and club draws (including some two-club combos). I want to extract value from worse pairs, Ax and broadways and charge club/straight draws, while keeping the pot controlled against the small portion of his range that has a made flush. Betting about half the pot accomplishes that: it denies free rivers to one-club hands, gets value from worse pairs and Ax, and keeps a reasonable fold equity. Plan if he check-raises big: he is likely polarized (flush/set) and I will strongly consider folding or pot-control depending on sizing; against a small raise I can call and reassess on the river. Therefore I will bet half pot (600).

Facing a 1/3 pot turn bet (600 into 1800) on a draw-heavy board now with three clubs (4c7c9c5s). As2c holds Ace-high with nut flush blocker (As blocks strong Ax flush draws) and Ace blocker to top of her value range (AA/AK/AQ unlikely as she'd bet bigger). Equity ~25-35% vs her polarized SB continuation range (strong value like sets/TPTK, flush draws, combo draws, some bluffs/air), easily crushes pot odds (4:1, need 20%). Called flop similarly for good odds; consistent sizing suggests not huge value (if monster, larger bet likely). OOP but deep effective stacks (~9k) allow river control/ realization of equity. Raising unnecessary (thin value, fold equity low vs aggro Minni who calls light per history e.g., Hand 29/73/115); folding too tight after pot-committing pre/flop OOP. Plan: call, see river (value bet/raise A-high if improves/brick, fold to massive jam if pure value shows). Maximizes EV long-term.

I hold K♠K♣ on 4♣-7♣-5♠-9♣-9♥. Elon called my flop and turn bets, so his range is mostly medium pocket pairs, broadway/Ax hands and club draws; the hands that beat me are primarily sets and made club flushes. A set (99/77/55/44) would have been a lot more likely to raise the turn, and while a flush is possible it’s a minority of his calling combos. Against the bulk of his calling range (QQ/JJ/TT/88, some A-high/broadway hands and missed draws) my kings are still ahead, so I want to extract value. I’ll bet around half the pot to get called by worse pairs and busted clubs while keeping reasonable fold equity: bet 1,200 into 2,400. If he check-raises big I’ll assume a very strong holding (flush/set) and fold or pot-control depending on sizing; I’ll call smaller raises and re-evaluate.

River decision: Pot 3600, facing 1200 bet (1/3 pot, ~4:1 odds, need ~20% equity). As2c holds A-high with As blocker to top of her value range (AA/AK/AQ unlikely given small sizing consistency across streets) and 2c blocker to some weak flushes/combos. Board (4c7c5s9c9h) very draw-heavy: flushes completed turn (she bet through), missed straight draws (68/36/8T), paired 9s hits some two-pair/set value but also bluffs/value thin bets from aggro SB range. History shows Minni aggressive pre/postflop (frequent raises/cbets, calls light e.g., H29/73/83/103/115), consistent with polarized range here (value: 99/97s/sets/flushes; bluffs: missed draws/A-high/air). Equity ~30-40% vs this (beats bluffs/missed A-high, live vs value); crushes pot odds. Called flop/turn similarly for odds/equity realization OOP vs deep aggro opponent; folding now inconsistent and -EV (sunk cost irrelevant, but range/line strength). No raise (thin value, low fold equity vs calling station tendencies). OOP control post-call irrelevant on river. Deep stacks (~8.8k eff post-call) fine ICM-wise mid-late tournament (118/150). Call maximizes EV, consistent with preflop wide defense plan.

Holding 3♣8♠ on the button — a very weak, unplayable offsuit hand. There’s only the blinds in front of me and the cost to see a flop is just 100 into a 150 pot, so calling is cheap and keeps me in position. I don’t want to open-raise to 200 with such a hand: it’s poorly playable postflop and likely dominated if the blinds defend or 3‑bet. Plan: call 100, see the flop with positional advantage, fold to strong aggression, or proceed cautiously with small C-bets on favorable textures.

Preflop in SB with 6s7s suited connectors. Minni limped from button, pot 250, to call 50 more (total 100). Excellent implied odds at 5:1, hand plays well multiway postflop with straight/flush potential. SB position is awkward but cheap price justifies calling to realize equity. Folding too tight, raising unnecessary vs button limp as it risks isolation OOP with speculative hand. Opponents deep-stacked, Minni aggressive but limp suggests possible weak/trappy range. Speculate for profit.

In Hand #119, I am in the Big Blind holding Jc 8d. Both Minni (Button) and Elon (Small Blind) have limped into the pot for 100 total. Since the action is checked to me and there is no raise to call, I have the option to check and see a free flop. J8o is a mediocre hand, but it has some potential in a multi-way limped pot. There is no reason to bloat the pot with a raise given my relatively short stack of 63.5 big blinds compared to Minni's massive stack. Checking allows me to realize my equity cheaply.

Flop is Ad Kc 8c, a dry but strong board with top pair top kicker potential (AK), strong aces, kings, and a club flush draw. My 6s7s has zero equity: no pair, no flush draw (spades), and only a weak gutshot to the 9 for a inferior straight (6789T at best). Pure air in SB OOP first to act. Preflop call was correct speculation with suited connectors multiway deep, but flop whiffed completely. No fold equity to a bet OOP vs button limper (wide range including Ax, suited broadways, pairs) and checking BB (trappy or wide). Bluffing here commits too much stack (pot 300, stacks deep but mine only 7650) and risks being iso'd by aggression from Minni who often cbets. Check to realize fold equity post-check or induce bluffs/value from worse, then fold to likely cbet. Pot control essential with nothing.

On a flop of Kc-Ad-8c, I have hit bottom pair with my Eight of Diamonds. The pot is small (300) and it is a limped pot. Elon has checked from the Small Blind. This board is very heavy with high cards (Ace and King) and a flush draw. While I have a pair, it is vulnerable and easily outkicked or beaten by any King, Ace, or better pair. However, in a multi-way limped pot, checking is the most prudent action to see how the Button (Minni) reacts and to avoid bloat with a marginal hand. I will check to see the turn for free.

I hold 3♣8♠ on a flop of 8♣ K♣ A♦ — I have a pair of eights (bottom pair) with a weak 3‑kicker and only a remote backdoor flush possibility (one club in my hand + two on board). This is a multiway pot with two opponents still to act; both checked the flop which shows weakness but their ranges still include Ax/Kx or stronger pairs that dominate me. Betting into two players with a marginal pair unnecessarily builds the pot and risks facing a raise or check‑raise from a hand that currently beats me. With deep stacks and positional advantage I prefer to pot‑control and realize equity. Plan: check and see a free turn; call small bets in position to re‑evaluate on the turn, but fold to significant aggression.

Turn board pairs the 8s (8c/8h), bringing possible trips, two pair combos (A8, K8, 88), and completing gutshots/straights like 56/79, plus any club flush draws from flop. My 67s still has zero equity: no pair, no flush draw (wrong suit), no straight draw (board blocks 89T for straights around 678, but I need specific cards). Pure air OOP first to act in SB multiway pot. All checked flop, suggesting caution or weakness across ranges: Minni (button limper, aggressive history) might have wide Ax/pairs/draws but checked back; Flash (BB) passive check. Pot tiny at 300 vs my 7650 stack. Betting as bluff risks iso from Minni (deep, loves aggression) or call from draws/value, low fold equity OOP vs two. No value, bad spot to bluff deep. Check to pot control, realize 0 equity cheaply, induce bluffs/value thin bets I can fold to, or see free river. Preflop speculation correct but board whiffed hard; no reason to commit further. Long-term EV: preserve stack for better spots.

In Hand #119, the turn has brought an Eight of Hearts, giving me three-of-a-kind (trips). The flop was checked around by all three players, which suggests that neither Elon nor Minni likely holds a strong Ace or King. Since I now hold a very strong hand on a board that looks safe, I will check again. This is a deceptive play designed to induce a bluff from Minni, who has shown aggression in previous hands, or to allow them to catch a second-best hand on the river that might pay off a value bet. Betting now might simply force folds from their likely weak ranges.

Turn gives me trips — 8♠ plus 8♣ and 8♥ on board is a very strong holding. Both opponents checked the flop and the turn which suggests weakness or pot control rather than strength (although hands that beat me — AA, KK, or A8/K8 full houses — are possible but less likely given passive lines). This is a good spot to start extracting value and charge any club-heavy draw that was live on the flop. I want a size that gets called by single-pair hands (Ax, Kx, medium pairs) and by draws, but that won’t overcommit me if I run into a big check‑raise. A bet of ~2× the pot (600 into 300) is a balanced, value/protection size here. If I get a small raise I’ll call and re-evaluate on the river; a large check‑raise would force me to carefully consider the likelihood of full houses and proceed cautiously.

Facing a 600 bet into 300 pot (now 900 total) on a paired board with possible trips, two pairs (A8, K8, 88), completed club flushes, and strong Ax/Kx value. My 67ss has zero showdown value or draws: no pair, no flush draw (spades miss clubs), no straight draw (board 88KA blocks relevant combos). Preflop limp-call was speculative with suited connectors deep multiway, but complete whiff on all streets. All checked flop indicating caution, but Minni (aggro button limper with 25k stack) fires turn, representing value or semi-bluff. Calling commits 600 for 2.5:1 with 0 equity, dead money OOP vs two opponents (Flash may still act post-call). Raising as bluff has low fold equity vs Minni's range (deep, calls light), poor vs BB squeeze potential. EV negative long-term; fold preserves 7650 stack for premium spots in late tournament stages (hand 119/150, 3 players left). Discipline over curiosity.

In Hand #119, the turn card (8h) has given me trips (three-of-a-kind Eights). After the flop was checked around by all players, the action on the turn is a significant overbet from Minni, who bets 600 into a 300-chip pot. This 2x pot sizing is highly polarized; it often represents either a very strong hand (like a full house or trips with a better kicker) or a total bluff attempting to steal a limped pot. Given that the hand was limped pre-flop and checked on the flop, it is less likely Minni holds a monster like A8 or K8, though they are possible. By calling instead of raising, I keep Minni's bluffing range wide and allow her to continue her aggression on the river, whereas a raise might force a fold from everything except hands that beat me. I will call the 600 and evaluate the river.

On the river, the board is 8c-Kc-Ad-8h-Ac. I hold 8d-Jc, giving me a full house: Eights full of Aces (8-8-8-A-A). However, this is a dangerous board because any player holding a single Ace now has a higher full house: Aces full of Eights (A-A-A-8-8). Minni limped pre-flop and checked the flop (A-K-8), which is a common way to play an Ace or a King in a limped pot. Her 2x pot overbet on the turn (8h) was highly polarized, usually representing either an Eight or a total bluff. If she has the final Eight, we chop. If she has an Ace, I am beaten. If she was bluffing, betting now might fold her out, whereas checking allows her to attempt one last bluff. Since I only beat a bluff and chop with another Eight, checking is the most prudent play to control the pot and potentially induce a bet from a weaker hand.

River: I hold 3♣8♠ on 8♣ K♣ A♦ 8♥ A♣. My hand is a full house (8s full of Aces: 8♠ + 8♣ + 8♥ + A♦ + A♣) — a very strong holding. The only hands that beat me are any opponent Ace (making aces full) or pocket Aces (quads). Flash’s line (check the flop, call my 600 turn, then check the river) is more consistent with Kx, a club/missed draw, or a medium pair than with a confident rivered Ace (although a trap is possible). With one opponent left I want to extract thin value from Kx, weaker pairs and missed draws while controlling the downside vs the small number of Ace combos. I will place a value bet of ~2/3 pot to achieve that. If Flash check-raises large (a shove) I will assume a very strong Ace/quads range and fold; if he makes a small raise I will call and re-evaluate.

On the river, the board has double-paired with Aces and Eights (8c-Kc-Ad-8h-Ac). My hole cards (8d-Jc) combined with the board give me a full house: Eights full of Aces (8-8-8-A-A). However, the second Ace on the river is a significant danger card, as any player holding a single Ace now has a superior full house: Aces full of Eights (A-A-A-8-8). Minni's line (limping pre-flop, checking the flop, overbetting the turn, and betting the river) is consistent with either an Eight or an Ace. If she has an Eight, we chop the pot. If she has an Ace, she has me beaten. Given the pot odds (calling 1000 to win a total pot of 3500) and the nature of a limped pot where she could be value-betting a King or even bluffing a missed club draw, I cannot justify folding a full house. I will call to realize my equity, though I expect a chop or a loss to an Ace more often than a win against a bluff.

Preflop on the button in a 3-handed pot with blinds posted (pot 150, to call 100). Holding Qs4h, an offsuit queen-high hand with poor connectivity and playability. Even in a short-handed game with position, this hand has negative EV for calling or raising due to domination risks, weak post-flop potential, and Minni's history of aggression from the blinds. Optimal play is to fold and preserve stack for better opportunities in the late stages of the tournament.

I have a premium hand, Ace-King offsuit, in the Small Blind. With the Button folding, I am heads-up against the Big Blind (Minni). I should raise to build the pot and take control of the hand. A standard raise to 300 (3x the big blind) is appropriate here to extract value and potentially win the blinds immediately.

Facing a small‑blind open to 300 heads‑up. Pot is 400 and I must call 200 (pot odds require ≈33% equity). I hold 2♦8♠ — a very weak, easily dominated hand with poor playability and I’ll be out of position postflop (SB is on the button). A light 3‑bet here with 2‑8o is too speculative (min‑raise to 500 still commits chips out of position and will often be called by better hands). With a large effective stack I should preserve chips for better spots — fold and wait for a more favorable situation.

I am on the button with Ten-Six offsuit. While 3-handed play warrants a wider opening range, this specific hand is quite weak (T6o). Both Minni and Elon have shown considerable aggression and a willingness to defend their blinds or 3-bet. With 30 hands remaining and a comfortable stack of 66 big blinds, I prefer to fold this marginal hand and wait for a better spot rather than risking chips on a low-probability steal attempt against active opponents.

Heads-up from the small blind with K♦5♥. In heads-up play the button/blinds are opened very wide — I want to take the initiative and use position postflop. K5o is marginal but has decent blocking value and plays tolerably in position. A minimum open to 200 applies pressure, preserves a lot of stack depth if Elon 3-bets, and maximizes fold equity vs his wide BB defending range. If he 3-bets heavily I’ll fold and wait for a better spot; if he calls I’ll play the flop aggressively in position. Raise to the min (200) to steal the blind and build the pot on my terms.

Preflop in BB facing minraise to 200 from SB after button folds. Hand is T2s, a suited connector with straight and flush potential. Excellent pot odds: calling 100 into 300 pot (3:1). SB range is wide for minraises, especially stealing vs BB. Defending wide OOP with playable hands like this is standard to realize equity. Folding too tight, raising too strong for this hand. Will call and play postflop cautiously, using position disadvantage but good implied odds vs Minni's aggressive stack.

Flop: 7h 7c 5c — I hold Kd5h: two pair (7s and 5s) with a King kicker. Heads‑up vs Elon (called my preflop min) this is a very strong holding; his calling range is wide (Axs, Kx, pocket pairs, suited connectors). Hands that beat me right now are any 7 (trips/quads) or some 5x combos (55, A5) — those are a minority of his calling range. Betting here extracts value from worse hands (A5, single 7 with worse kicker, overcards that will float, club draws) and also denies free cards that could help two‑card club draws or turn 7/5 improvements. I want a bet size big enough to get value but not so large I fold out all worse hands — I will lead for value with a pot‑ish bet of 600. If he calls I proceed and re‑evaluate on the turn; if he check‑raises large it will often indicate trips/full house and I will re‑assess (call small/medium raises for pot odds, be cautious to folding to an obvious shove).

Preflop call with Td2d suited was speculative for implied odds and multi-way potential, but flop 7h7c5c completely misses my hand: no pair, no straight draw (gapped), no flush draw (wrong suits). Board favors pairs, sets (77,75s,57s), flush draws (clubs), and overpairs. Minni's minraise pre and 60% pot bet flop suggests strong range including value hands that crush me and semi-bluffs with equity. My equity is negligible (~10-20% vs polarized range), pot odds 600 to 1600 require ~37% - not met. OOP calling commits too much stack (8% of my 7450) into a pot where future streets likely worse. Raising air OOP risks stack-off vs better hands. Minni aggressive historically (frequent raises/bets), so fold preserves stack for better spots in late tournament phase (hand 121/150). Standard fold.

Holding J♦2♠ on the button in a 3-handed pot is one of the weakest playable hands — offsuit J2 has poor playability and is heavily dominated by typical defending ranges from the small blind and big blind. Both opponents have shown willingness to defend and raise preflop, so an open-raise will often get called or 3‑bet, leaving me in difficult postflop spots with a marginal holding. Limping/calling 100 just sows a multiway pot with little equity and no fold equity. With a healthy stack (25,950) and many hands left, I’ll preserve my chips and wait for a better spot rather than gamble with a junk hand. Fold.

84o is a trash hand with poor playability, low equity, and no straight or flush potential that justifies entering the pot. In small blind vs big blind, out of position post-flop, folding unopened pots (button folded) is standard to preserve stack in late tournament stages with 74BB effective stacks. No need to defend wide here as it risks spewing chips unnecessarily against Flash's likely wide BB calling range.

3-handed on the button first to act pre-flop with Qd4s offsuit. This is a marginal hand at best: queen-high with a weak 4 kicker, no suited or connected value for speculative play. In short-handed play, button opens wide, but Q4o is below standard opening range due to poor post-flop playability, gets dominated by Qx frequently, and struggles multi-way against Minni's deep stack and aggressive tendencies observed in history (frequent pre-flop raises and calls). Folding preserves stack for better spots; limping is suboptimal, raising risks playing OOP post-flop poorly. EV-maximizing fold.

I am in the Small Blind with K7o. The Button has folded, making this a blind-vs-blind battle against Minni, who has a significant chip lead. K7o is a strong enough hand to open-raise in this spot to try and take down the blinds immediately. While Minni has been active and has position post-flop, folding is too conservative and just calling (limping) allows her to realize her equity for free or raise me off the hand easily. I will raise to 300 to put pressure on the Big Blind.

Facing a 3bb open from the SB with 2♥7♥ — a very weak hand. I won't 3‑bet with junk; it's cheap to defend and the SB is likely opening a wide range. I call the additional 200 (300 total) to see a flop, realize backdoor heart/straight equity, and keep pot size controllable.
