Postflop I'll fold to strong aggression on unfavorable boards and look to exploit weak continuation bets or favorable textures.

The flop Ac-Qs-Js is a very heavy board that likely connects with much of the Big Blind's calling range, including Aces, Queens, Jacks, and various straight draws. While I have a gutshot straight draw with my King, betting into this board out of position against the chip leader is risky. I will check to control the size of the pot and see how Minni reacts, potentially realizing my equity for free or evaluating a call if she bets a small amount.

I have 2♥7♥ on an A♣ Q♠ J♠ flop. I have no pair and no realistic draw: with zero hearts on the flop a heart flush is now impossible and there’s no straight equity. The texture heavily favors the SB raiser’s preflop range (A/Q/J and broadway combos, plus spade holdings). Flash checked, which could be weakness or a control/slow-play, but I have virtually no equity and no strong blocker to credible value hands. Pot is small (600) and my stack is deep — no reason to create unnecessary risk by bluffing OOP. Plan: check and give up to any bet; if checked through I’ll re-evaluate on the turn but won’t invest without a real improvement or clear read.

The turn pairs the Queen on an already very coordinated board. Since both of us checked the flop, it's possible neither of us has a strong piece of this board, but it favors the Big Blind's calling range. I still have a gutshot straight draw with my King. I will check again to try and see a free river or evaluate a small bet from Minni.

I have 2♥7♥ — no pair, no straight/flush draw and effectively zero equity on A♣ Q♠ J♠ Q♣. Flash was the preflop raiser and checked the turn; that check can be either pot-control or a slow-play with a real hand (A, Qx, Jx, two-pair/set). With deep stacks and a small pot (600) there is no reason to bluff OOP into a preflop raiser who can easily call or raise with value. I’ll avoid creating unnecessary risk: check and fold to any turn bet. If it checks through I’ll re-evaluate on the river only if I improve or get a clear read.

On this board of Ac-Qs-Js-Qc-9h, my King-high has almost no showdown value. However, the action has been check-check-check since the flop. This suggests that Minni likely hasn't connected strongly with the board either, or she is playing very cautiously with the chip lead. While I could try to represent a straight or a slow-played Ace with a river bet, Minni has shown a willingness to call down in the past, and she has plenty of chips to spare. Betting into the chip leader on a board this coordinated with only King-high is a low-percentage play. I will check and hope for a check-back, though I expect to lose to any small pair or a Jack. If she bets, I will fold.

Board is A♣ Q♠ J♠ Q♣ 9♥; I hold 2♥7♥. The river 9♥ gives me no pair and no meaningful draw (only three hearts total — no flush). Flash was the preflop raiser and has checked every street, which can be either pot‑control with strong holdings (A, Qx, Jx, two‑pair) or a slowplay — I have almost zero showdown equity against that range. The pot is small (600) and both stacks are deep, so attempting a bluff OOP here is high variance with low fold equity against a caller who can comfortably call a half‑pot/three‑quarters bet. I’ll check and preserve my stack; I’ll fold to any river bet.

I am on the button with Kh Qs, a strong starting hand in 3-handed play. With nearly 70 big blinds, I have plenty of room to play. My strategy here is to open for a standard 3x raise to take control of the pot and pressure the blinds. Minni has a large chip lead and has been active, while Elon is close to me in stack size, so I need to be cautious but assertive.

I’m in the small blind with pocket 5s facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, I need to add 250). Stacks are very deep (256bb), so this is an ideal set-mining spot — the call is cheap relative to the potential implied odds. A tiny 3-bet to 500 would be marginal (too small to fold out hands and invites 4-bets) and I don’t want to bloat the pot OOP versus two players unnecessarily. I’ll call to see a flop; if I hit a set I’ll look to extract, otherwise I’ll proceed cautiously out of position and fold to heavy aggression when appropriate.

Hand 124, BB with J7s suited connectors. Pot 700, call 200 more (pot odds ~3.5:1 direct, better implied). Flash button raise likely wide (steal range). Minni SB call after raise suggests speculative or medium strength, but positionally awkward so capped somewhat. 3-handed, stacks: me 7300 (~73BB), Flash 6650 (~66BB), Minni deep 25350. J7s plays well multiway: flush draw, gutshots, pairs + draw potential. Implied odds strong vs deep Minni. Raising risks multiway action or isolation vs shorter Flash without domination. Folding too tight in BB defense spot. Call cheap, realize equity postflop with decent position on SB. History shows Minni aggressive postflop but pays off draws; exploit with speculative hand.

I called preflop as a set-mining spot. I missed — flop A‑8‑7 rainbow is terrible for an underpair: only two outs to set, no meaningful backdoors, and both the button raiser and the BB continue with lots of Ax/overpair/overcard combos. I’m out of position and have almost no fold equity with two opponents and deep stacks, so building the pot is wrong. Check to control size and preserve my stack; plan to fold to any real continuation bet. If both check back I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (only continue if I improve or see clear weakness).

Flop: Ah 8c 7d gives me bottom pair (sevens) with jack kicker and backdoor heart flush draw. Multiway pot (900) after all calling preflop, Minni SB checked first (likely weak or trapping, but her pre-call suggests speculative/medium strength capped by position). Flash button raiser has wide range (Ax, broadway, suited connectors, bluffs); expects to c-bet frequently. My hand has decent showdown value, some equity realization OOP vs button but IP vs SB, and implied odds vs deep Minni stacks. No fold equity to raise multiway without top pair. Betting risks check-raise from SB or button isolation; too thin for value, invites overcards. Check controls pot, invites button c-bet (which I can call profitably with ~25-30% equity vs c-bet range), realizes equity passively, and keeps pot small for backdoor potential. Folding pair too tight 3-handed deep. Prior history: opponents bluff/aggressive postflop, exploitable by check-calling.

In this 3-way pot on an Ace-high board, my King-high has very little showdown value, but it is also difficult to bluff both opponents successfully, especially since Minni has a massive stack and has shown a willingness to call. As the pre-flop aggressor, I could represent the Ace with a continuation bet, but checking back allows me to see a turn card for free and keep the pot manageable. If I improve to a pair of Kings or Queens on the turn, I may have the best hand, though I would still need to be cautious of an Ace. Checking is the most prudent play to preserve my stack in a 3-way situation where the board texture favors many potential calling ranges.

I called preflop as a set-mining spot and missed — I still haven’t improved on the turn (board: 8c 7d Ah 8h). With two opponents left and out of position, I have almost no fold equity and shouldn’t bloat the pot with a bluff. Both players checked the flop and the turn, which looks like pot control/weakness rather than giving me a clear fold target. I’ll check back to keep the pot manageable and only continue if I actually improve or face a tiny, peelable bet on the river; I’ll fold to any significant aggression from either opponent.

Turn board pairs the 8h, completing the nut heart flush draw (A♥8♥7♥J♥) while holding bottom pair (77 with 7♦7♥) for 10+ outs to nuts plus showdown value against weaker Ax, 8x, 7x, and incomplete draws (e.g., 9T, 56). Pot 900, checked through flop (Flash whiffed c-bet with button range likely including Ax, broadway, suited connectors, bluffs), Minni checks again OOP suggesting capped speculative/medium strength from preflop call. 3-handed deep (71BB effective vs Flash, deeper vs Minni), prior history shows opponents bluff/aggressive postflop but passive here—exploit by betting for thin value, charging villain draws (straights, weaker pairs), protecting, denying equity realization, and building pot with massive equity (~50%+ vs calling ranges). Check risks free river card to Ax/8x outs or Flash check-raise trap; bet size 2/3 pot balances value/protection without overbetting paired board. Avoids check-fold weak, check-call exploitable multiway.

I currently hold King-high with no made hand and no draws on a board featuring an Ace and a pair of Eights. After everyone checked the flop, Elon has led out for 600 into a 900 pot. Given that there is still another player behind me (Minni) and I have no real equity to continue, calling is not a viable option. Attempting a bluff raise against two opponents, one of whom has a massive stack, is too risky. Folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my chips.

Fold. I called preflop as a set-mining spot and checked the flop to control the pot — the turn lead changes things. I have pocket 5s (an underpair to both the Ace and the paired 8). I have only 2 outs to make a set on the river (2/46 ≈ 4.35% equity). Pot math: calling 600 into a 1,500 pot (600 to win 2,700 after my call) requires ≈22.2% equity to break even. My raw equity to improve is far below that, and there are no realistic backdoor ways to win more than a tiny percent of the time. Raising as a bluff OOP here is also poor — I have almost no fold equity versus a player who leads into two checkers and would simply call or shove with value. Given the bet size, the action (check-check on flop then lead on turn) and my lack of meaningful outs, folding preserves a healthy stack for better spots.

Raise to 300. K♣J♣ on the button is a strong, playable hand with great positional advantage — I want to take the initiative, isolate the blinds, and collect the blinds when they fold. A 3xBB-ish open (300) is the table norm and gives the right balance of fold equity and postflop maneuverability with both opponents deep. Plan: if called, use position to c-bet favorable K/J/club or connected boards, exercise pot control on A‑high boards, and fold to large, committed 3-bets; if both fold I pick up the blinds.

Preflop in SB with A9o facing a button raise to 300 from Minni, who has been very aggressive throughout the history, often raising from late positions with wide ranges. Pot is 450, to call 250 for great odds (~1.8:1), but OOP postflop vs a deep-stacked loose button. A9o has good equity vs a wide button opening range (~35-40%), blocks AA/AK, and plays well as a 3-bet bluff/c value mix. BB (Flash) has a decent stack but folds often to aggression. Raising to 900 (3x the raise size) applies pressure, denies equity to worse hands, and gets value from worse aces/broadways Minni might call with. Folding too tight; flat calling risks being OOP dominated postflop. With 80BB effective, sizing up to fold out marginal calls and set up postflop if called.

I have pocket Queens, a premium starting hand. Minni opened from the button and Elon 3-bet from the small blind. I should 4-bet here to take control of the pot and maximize value against hands like AK, JJ, or TT. A raise to 2300 sets up a potential all-in on the flop or against a 5-bet shove, which I am prepared to call given our stack sizes and the hand's strength.

I opened to 300 with KcJc on the button, but Elon 3‑bet to 900 and Flash 4‑bet to 2300. Pot is 3500 and I must call 2000 more (total 2300) — that gives me immediate pot odds of 2000/(3500+2000) ≈ 36.4% to break even. KJs is a good hand, but multiway facing a 3‑bet + a 4‑bet it is often dominated by polarized 4‑bet ranges (QQ+/AK) and my equity multiway will typically be below the required ~36%. Flash’s 4‑bet to 2300 is a significant, committed sizing from a 43 BB stack and Elon’s earlier 3‑bet signals strength as well. Raising or shoving here with KJs would be high variance and ill‑advised; calling to see a flop multiway against two likely strong ranges is also marginal. I already invested only 300 chips — folding preserves a large stack and follows my plan to fold to large, committed 3‑bets/4‑bets. Fold and wait for a clearer spot in position.

Facing a large 3-bet jam from Flash in the BB after Minni's fold. Flash's squeeze range is strong: QQ+, AK, AQ+. A9o blocks AA/AK but has only ~30-35% equity against that, and postflop OOP with shallow effective stacks (Flash ~43BB, me ~71BB) is disastrous for a marginal ace. Pot odds ~2.5:1 not enough given reverse implied odds and fold equity denied. Calling commits ~20% stack to a tough spot; 4-betting risks cooler or forces off worse. Standard fold to preserve stack for superior positions/hand strengths in late tournament.

3-handed on the button with a deep effective stack (71BB). Tc7d is a playable hand in an extremely wide button opening range, roughly top 60% or better 3-handed. The blinds are relatively tight-passive based on history (many folds to button aggression), and there's 150 in dead money to steal. Raising steals blinds frequently or builds pot in position post-flop where we have initiative. Standard sizing is 3x BB (300). Folding wastes position; limping bad out of position OOP. Raising maximizes EV.

I am in the Small Blind with 9d 8c, which is a decent connector but facing a raise from Elon on the Button. Elon has shown aggression recently, but calling from the Small Blind puts me out of position for the rest of the hand against a player who has been active. Given my stack size and the relative strength of my hand, 98-offsuit is not strong enough to call a 3x raise out of position, especially with Minni in the Big Blind yet to act. Folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

Facing a button open to 300 and Flash has folded, I’m heads-up out of the big blind with 7♥2♥ — essentially the worst playable hand. The pot is 450 and calling 200 gives me ~30.8% pot odds, but 7-2s has very poor equity vs a button open and plays horribly out of position (often dominated and difficult to realize equity). I could consider an exploitative 3-bet given my deep stack and Elon’s aggression, but 7-2s is too weak to do that profitably. Prefer to preserve my large stack and wait for a much better spot. Fold.

On the button with T8o, I have a semi-decent hand to open in a three-handed game. My stack is roughly 78 big blinds, similar to Elon's, while Minni is the massive chip leader. Opening for a standard 3x (300) allows me to take control of the hand from the best position. Given the blinds' playstyles, I expect to take the blinds down fairly often or play a pot in position if called.

I have 9♦9♥ in the small blind facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, 250 to call). With deep stacks and a frequent 300 BTN open, pocket nines are a strong value hand but vulnerable if allowed multiway and out of position. I want to isolate the button and take the initiative rather than limp/call into a multiway, so I’ll 3-bet for value and fold equity. A raise to 1200 (total) is large enough to fold out many speculative hands and get value from worse pairs/broadways, yet leaves plenty of room post‑flop and doesn’t overcommit the stack. If Elon 4-bets big I’ll re-evaluate, but this sizing maximizes our chance to win the pot preflop or play HU with position advantage against a wide BTN range.

Qh8s is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability, dominated equity against typical raising ranges from Flash on the button and Minni's 3-bet from the small blind. Facing a 12BB raise after a button open, the pot odds (calling 1100 into 1600) do not justify entering with such marginal holdings, as post-flop we'll be out of position against strong ranges. Minni's stack and history suggest strength or position play; folding preserves stack for better spots in late tournament.

Minni has made a large 3-bet to 1200, which is four times my opening raise. While I have the advantage of position on the button, Ten-Eight offsuit is a weak hand to defend against such a significant raise, especially from the tournament chip leader. Minni has been playing aggressively with her stack, and T8o is likely dominated by her 3-betting range. Calling would commit a significant portion of my stack to a pot where I am likely behind and would face further pressure on later streets. Folding allows me to preserve my 72BB stack and wait for a better opportunity.

Button with A♣4♣ — a suited ace with position on both blinds. Pot is 150, to-call 100 and min-raise is 200. I want to seize initiative and use position to play postflop, plus exploit the shorter stacks in the blinds. A standard 3xBB open (to 300) gives good fold equity, keeps the pot manageable against their ~7k stacks, and preserves postflop maneuverability with backdoor club/ace-high play. If I face a shove/3-bet I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing, but opening to 300 is the +EV play here.

K4o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability out of position in the small blind. Facing a raise to 300 from Minni on the button, who has been aggressive throughout the history and has a massive stack (25k+). Pot odds are about 1.8:1 (250 to call for 700 effective pot including call), but equity against Minni's raising range is low (~25-30% vs typical button open), and multiway with BB behind worsens it. No position postflop, vulnerable to 3-bets or BB involvement. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots in late tournament stage (hand 128/150).

I am holding AJ offsuit in the Big Blind, which is a premium hand in a three-handed game. Minni, the chip leader, has raised from the button, which is a wide opening range. I want to take the initiative and maximize the value of my hand rather than just calling and playing out of position. A 3-bet to 1,000 chips puts pressure on Minni's button range and allows me to take down the pot immediately or play a controlled pot with the lead.

I opened to 300 on the button with A♣4♣ and Flash (BB) 3‑bet to 1000. With position, a suited Ace and a blocker to other A‑hands, the best line is to flat-call and keep the pot manageable — I can use positional advantage and postflop play to realize equity and apply pressure. A small reraise to 1700 won’t fold out many better hands and will bloat the pot against a shorter stack (Flash ~6500) where a shove or call are likely; I’d rather see a flop and play with initiative. Plan: call 700 more (1000 total committed), continue selectively on club/ace or favourable textures, and re‑evaluate facing large turn/river aggression or a shove.

I have flopped top pair with the best possible kicker (TPTK) on a board that is relatively disconnected. My 3-bet pre-flop represents a strong range, and this flop favors my range significantly. I will lead out with a continuation bet of approximately 40% of the pot to extract value from middle pairs (like 77-TT), weaker Jacks, and potential straight draws (like 78s or 89s), while also protecting my equity against overcards.

Call 800. Flash opened a small c-bet (800 into a ~2850 pot) after 3-betting pre. With A♣4♣ I have a useful ace-blocker, backdoor club potential and position — and the call is cheap relative to the pot (you only need ~18% equity to break even). Raising here bloats a pot facing a shorter-stacked, likely polarized 3‑bettor and will often get you committed with a mediocre hand. I’ll call to realize equity, use position on the turn to apply pressure when I pick up a club or an ace (or when the turn is a safe card to bluff), and fold/peel selectively to large river aggression if I don’t improve.

I have top pair with an Ace kicker (AJ) on a board of J-6-5-5. The turn 5d pairs the board. While this could potentially give Minni trips if she's holding a 5 (like A5s or 65s), it is more likely she has a weaker Jack or a pocket pair like 77-TT. I will continue with a second barrel to extract value from those hands and to protect my equity against any straight draws (like 78). A bet of 1,500 into a 3,650 pot is a standard sized continuation bet that keeps me in control of the action.

Heads‑up vs Flash (the BB) on a 6♥‑5♣‑J♦‑5♦ board with A♣4♣. I still have only Ace‑high plus a tiny backdoor club possibility and I block a chunk of his Ace combos. Flash 3‑betted pre and barreled flop and turn, so his range is weighted to Jx, medium/overpairs and some sets, with a few bluffs. He bet 1,500 into a 5,150 pot — calling 1,500 gives me ~23% break‑even equity, and with position I can realize equity or fold to clear river aggression.

Raising here is thin: it bloats the pot with a marginal hand, won’t fold many of his value hands, and risks a shove or a commit against better holdings. Folding would be too nitty given the price and our positional advantage. The +EV line is to call, keep the pot manageable, see the river, and re‑evaluate with the information from his river action. Calling 1,500 (3300 total committed).

The river card is a three, which is a blank and does not change the board texture significantly. I have top pair with the best possible kicker (Jacks and Fives with an Ace kicker). Given the small stack-to-pot ratio—I have 4,200 chips remaining and the pot is 6,650—I am committed to the hand. Minni's calls on the flop and turn suggest she holds a Jack or a medium pocket pair. By betting all-in, I maximize value from her range of Jacks, such as KJ or QJ, which are likely to call given the pot odds and her significant chip lead.

Heads‑up on the river vs Flash (BB) who’s just put his remaining stack in. I hold A♣4♣ on 6♥‑5♣‑J♦‑5♦‑3♥. Pot is 10,850 and it costs 4,200 to call. If I call the final pot will be 15,050, so I need ~4,200/15,050 ≈ 27.9% equity to break even. Flash 3‑bet pre and barreled flop and turn before shoving river — that line is heavily weighted to value (JJ, 66, 55, 33, strong overpairs and some Jx/two‑pair/full house combinations). The missed‑draw bluffs that he could have (club draws, some suited broadway floats) exist but are limited, and I block a number of Ace/club bluff combos by holding A♣4♣. My actual equity with mere Ace‑high versus this polarized, value‑heavy shove is well below the ~28% needed (realistic estimate ~10–20%). With a large effective stack advantage and many better spots to extract +EV, folding preserves edge; calling here would be a speculative, negative‑EV play. I fold.

74o is a complete trash hand with no straight or flush potential, gapped, offsuit, and low cards. Position on button after presumed SB fold and BB check (pot 150, to call 100 includes dead money), but even with dead money and position, this hand has zero playability postflop and is easily dominated. BB Minni plays aggressively in past hands and likely defends wide. With deep stacks (71bb effective) and 21 hands left in tournament, preserve stack for premium spots rather than risk entering with worst hand in range. Standard fold.

With Elon folding on the button, I am heads-up against Minni in the big blind. Queen-Ten offsuit is a strong hand in this blind vs. blind scenario. I will raise to 300 to take control of the pot and potentially win the blinds immediately, or set up a strong continuation on the flop if called.

I’m in the big blind with Q♦2♦ facing a 300 open from the small blind (button). Calling costs 200 into a 400 pot, which requires ~33% equity — Q2s is very often dominated by the SB’s opening range and will struggle to realize equity out of position. Three-betting as a bluff with Q2s is too thin (and I don’t want to bloating a pot OOP against the button), so I’ll preserve my deep stack and wait for a better spot. Fold.

Holding King-Jack offsuit on the button in a three-handed game is a very strong position. KJo is well within my opening range here. I will raise to the standard 3x (300 chips) to put pressure on the blinds and aim to take control of the pot early or play it in position post-flop.

Button opened to 300 (pot 450); I’m in the small blind with Q♦2♦ and must call 250. Q2s is a marginal/off-suit-queen type holding but it’s suited so it has some playability. Calling 250 is cheap relative to my deep stack and lets me defend vs what is likely a wide BTN opening range. I don’t want to 3-bet here — hand is easily dominated and I’d be out of position postflop. Plan: call to 300 total, fold to a meaningful preflop 3-bet, and postflop play straightforwardly — continue only if I hit a queen, make two pair/set, or have a strong diamond draw; otherwise pot-control or fold to pressure.

76o is a marginal hand at best, offsuit and low-connected. Facing a button raise from Flash (likely wide range) and a small blind call from Minni (tighter calling range), equity is insufficient postflop OOP against two players. Pot odds are ~3.5:1 (22% needed), but realized equity lower due to multiway pot and poor playability. Deep stacks, better to fold and wait for stronger hands with 20 hands left.

Check. My preflop plan was to only continue if I hit a Q, made two pair/set, or had a real diamond draw — none of those happened. With Q♦2♦ on T♦ J♥ 5♣ I only have Q-high and a backdoor (runner-runner) diamond possibility, and I’m out of position to the BTN who opened preflop; this flop connects well with his raising range so a C-bet OOP is low EV. I’ll check for pot control and to let him show aggression. Plan if he bets: fold to a large c-bet (~>1/2 pot, ≈350+) and consider calling small probes (≈≤200–250) to float only if I think I can realize equity or take it away on later streets; if he checks back I’ll take a free turn and continue only if I pair the Q or pick up a meaningful diamond draw.

I have top pair with a King kicker on a board of Ten-Jack-Five. This is a very strong hand three-handed. Minni has checked to me after calling from the small blind. I will make a continuation bet of 400 into the 700 pot to extract value from draws (like King-Queen or Queen-Nine) and middle pairs (like pocket Nines or Tens), while also protecting my hand against potential overcards on the turn.

I stick to my preflop / flop plan and fold. I have Q♦2♦ and after checking the flop (T♦ J♥ 5♣) I only have Q-high and a backdoor diamond draw that requires both turn+river (very low, ~4%). Flash’s bet of 400 into the ~700 pot is a sizeable c-bet (≈57% of the pot); calling 400 to win ~1500 requires ~26.7% equity. My realistic chances to improve (pair the Q by river ≈12.4% plus the tiny backdoor flush ≈4%) are well below the pot-odds threshold, and I’m out of position with a hand that’s easily dominated by the BTN’s c-betting range. No reason to bluff-raise OOP here — preserving a large stack and waiting for a better spot is higher EV. Action: fold.

9♣4♣ is a marginal but playable hand in 3‑handed play — suited, with decent implied odds and good playability in position. On the button I want to seize initiative and apply pressure to the blinds rather than limp-call and play a multiway pot out of position. A standard open to 300 (small, familiar sizing here) gives reasonable fold equity while keeping the pot manageable relative to deep stacks. Plan: fold to a big 3‑bet, otherwise continue in position — c‑bet on dry boards, pursue club/straight equity when favorable and control pot size on connected/dangerous boards.

A2 offsuit is a weak hand from the small blind facing a button raise from an aggressive Minni. It has poor postflop playability, is easily dominated by better aces, and doesn't block enough strong hands. Pot odds are decent (calling 250 into 450), but folding preserves my stack for better spots late in the tournament with 20 hands left. No need to defend marginally here against a wide button range.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Four suited. The button (Minni) has raised to 300. With 200 more to call into a pot of 450, I am getting 2.25:1 odds. K4s is a solid defending hand in this spot, especially since it has flush potential and some high card value. While I could 3-bet as a bluff, calling allows me to see a flop and realize my equity without bloating the pot against the current chip leader.

I have flopped the second-nut flush draw (King-high) along with a gutshot straight draw (needing a Ten) and one overcard. The board (9s-8h-Jh) is very coordinated and likely hits a lot of Minni's button opening range (straight draws, pairs, two-pairs). As the Big Blind, my standard play is to check to the pre-flop aggressor. If Minni bets, I have more than enough equity to continue, and I can evaluate whether to call or check-raise based on the bet sizing. For now, checking is the best way to control the pot while looking to realize my significant equity.

Flop: J♥ 9♠ 8♥. I hold 9♣4♣ — a single pair of nines with a poor kicker. Flash checked, and this board is fairly connected and two‑hearted: it hits a lot of the BB’s calling range (Jx, Q10/10-7 straights, many broadways and connectors). Important detail: there are zero clubs on the flop so my two clubs cannot make a flush by the river (I can’t get three clubs on the board any more), so my hand’s improvement outs are limited (mainly the remaining nines).

Given the vulnerability of middle pair here and the desire to control the pot vs a wide caller, I’m not going to lead. Checking preserves stack/chip equity, lets me use position to see a turn card, and allows me to call reasonable bets to realize equity or fold to heavy aggression. If Flash bets a small-to-medium amount I’ll call and re-evaluate on the turn; if he bombs it or raises strongly I’ll fold and preserve my deep stack. If he checks back I’ll take a free turn card and potentially take the lead on safe cards.

On the turn, the deuce of clubs is a complete blank that doesn't change the board texture. After the flop went check-check, Minni's range likely consists of weak pairs, missed high cards, or draws she didn't want to bet on a coordinated board. By betting here, I can take the pot down immediately against her air. If she calls, I still have significant equity with my King-high flush draw and gutshot straight draw to hit on the river. I will lead for approximately two-thirds of the pot to put pressure on her marginal holdings.

Call 450. I have a single pair of 9s with a terrible kicker on J‑9‑8 / 2 board — a vulnerable hand but the turn bet is small (~40% pot) and gives me good pot odds to see the river. There is no way for my clubs to make a flush by the river (only one club on board), so my improvement outs are limited (mainly the two remaining nines), but Flash checked the flop and is now firing a modest probe — his range can include lots of bluffs, thin value (8x, 9x, Jx), and missed overcards. Folding here would be conceding too much with a hand that has showdown value; raising is thin and will mostly get called or raised by better hands (Jx, Q10/straights), which would commit me with a poor kicker. I’ll call 450, control the pot, and re‑evaluate on the river (fold to heavy action if the river is hostile; call/small value bet if the river is safe).

I have completed a King-high flush on the river with the Ten of hearts. This is a very strong hand, only losing to the Ace-high flush. The board is quite connected (8-9-T-J), meaning Minni could easily have a straight (any Queen or Seven) or a lower flush that she will be unwilling to fold. Since the flop went check-check and she only called a turn bet, her range is likely comprised of one-pair hands, straights, or missed draws. A bet of approximately two-thirds of the pot is a standard value sizing here to maximize profit from her calling range while minimizing loss in the unlikely event she holds the Ace of hearts.

Call 1000. The pot is 2550 and Flash's river bet is 1000, so I'm getting roughly 1000/(2550+1000) ≈ 28% pot odds. I hold a vulnerable middle pair (9♣4♣) with a terrible kicker on J♥ T♥ 9♠ 2♣ Th board — my clean improvement outs are very limited and there are obvious hands that beat me (made hearts/straights/Jx/Tx and 9x with a better kicker). That said Flash checked the flop and made a small turn stab (450) before a modest river bet; that line is consistent with a fairly wide river betting range that includes bluffs and missed heart draws as well as thin value. Folding here would concede too much with a hand that still wins often enough given the price; raising is thin and will mainly get called or 3‑bet by better hands. I will call 1000 to see showdown and fold to a larger raise (if any).

3-handed on the button facing a probable SB fold and BB post (to call 100 into 150 pot). K8s is a strong suited hand with good playability, position, and equity against BB calling range. Raising steals blinds frequently, isolates if called, and sets up postflop aggression. Standard sizing around 3x BB. Minni BB raises often preflop but defends variably; Flash deep but likely out. Goal to maximize stack late tournament, aggression from position key.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Queen suited, which is a premium hand in a 3-handed game. Elon, the Button, has raised to 300. With a stack of 13,100, I want to isolate the raiser and build the pot. Calling would allow the Big Blind (Minni) to enter cheaply or potentially squeeze. A 3-bet to 1,000 is a standard sizing that puts pressure on Elon's opening range and takes initiative in the hand.

I’m in the big blind with Q♥T♣ facing a button open to 300 and an SB 3-bet to 1000 (pot 1400, I must call 900). My stack is deep (~198bb), so I can play post-flop. QTo is too weak to 4-bet for value and too marginal to isolate; folding is overly tight given the small cost to see a flop and the implied odds if I hit. Calling 900 (total commit 1000) keeps the pot manageable, preserves stack flexibility, and lets me see a flop where QT has decent playability (top pair, two-pair, straights, and broadway/straight/flush draw potential). Plan: call to see the flop; if Elon folds I’ll play heads-up versus Flash and continue with connected flops or strong draws; if it becomes 3-way I’ll proceed cautiously and fold to heavy aggression on dry boards unless I’ve connected well.

3-handed late in tournament (hand 132/150), on button with 66BB effective stack holding K8s suited - strong speculative hand with position, broadway potential, flush draw equity. Facing SB 3bet to 1000 after my 300 open (common wide 3bet range from SB including bluffs, Ax, pairs), BB flat calls 900 indicating possible medium strength/draws but not super premium. Pot 2300, calling additional 700 (~23% pot) offers excellent implied odds and direct odds (~4:1 when closing action). Folding too tight given playability and position; raising risks cooler multiway or fold equity not worth variance with deeper stacks. Call to realize equity postflop in position, control pot or attack weakness. Consistent with aggressive BTN strategy from history while balancing.

I am the pre-flop aggressor in a 3-way 3-bet pot. The board is very dry (3c 5d 5s), which theoretically favors a 3-bettor's range, but playing 3-way reduces the success rate of a continuation bet. With Ace-Queen suited, I have two overcards and a backdoor flush draw. Checking here allows me to see how both Elon (the original raiser) and Minni (the caller in the Big Blind) react. If it checks around, I can potentially realize my equity or bet on favorable turns. If there is a bet, I can evaluate based on the sizing and the action between the other two players.

I missed the flop with Q♥T♣ on 3c-5d-5s. This is a dry, low paired board and we’re in a 3‑way pot where Flash was the preflop 3‑bettor (likely a strong range) and Elon just called — so continuation‑bet bluffs into two opponents are thin. I have no pair and only two overcards, so I want pot control and to see how the other two act. Checking preserves stack flexibility, lets Elon act (and possibly fold if he missed), and avoids bloating the pot out of position with a marginal hand. Plan: check and fold to significant aggression unless I get clear indication of weakness or favorable odds to continue; if checks back, reevaluate on the turn and only barrel with a real improvement (Q/T) or a strong read.

Late in tournament (hand 132/150, 3-handed), on button with Kc8c facing checks from SB (Flash, 3bet preflop) and BB (Minni, flat caller). Board 5-5-3 rainbow is dry and paired, favoring my range as preflop raiser. Holding strong club flush draw (Kc8c + 3c), king blocker, no showdown but excellent equity (~35-45% multiway vs calling ranges). Both opponents checking indicates weakness: Flash may have missed 3bet range (Ax no 5/A, bluffs, mid pairs); Minni flatted wide but checks suggest draw/miss. Betting exploits this, semi-bluffs fold out A-high, weak pairs, gutshots (4x,6x), charges draws (e.g., 46,67), gets value from pocket pairs below KK or floated hands. Position allows fold equity and postflop control. Size 2/3 pot (2000) builds pot for when I hit while denying equity. Checking back too passive, risks free rivers to their equity/draws, poor given initiative and stacks (~60BB effective). Consistent with aggressive BTN play maximizing stack.

In Hand #132, I am in a 3-way 3-bet pot with Ace-Queen of clubs. The flop is 5-5-3, which is a very dry, paired board. I checked to the original raiser (Elon) and the Big Blind (Minni). Elon has bet 2,000 into a 3,000 pot. This bet is significant, representing roughly half of his remaining stack. While Ace-high can often be the best hand here, the presence of a third player (Minni) still to act behind me makes calling extremely risky. If I call and Minni has a pocket pair or a five, I am drawing thin and potentially getting squeezed. Furthermore, Elon's sizing into two players on this board suggests he either has a made hand (like an overpair) or is prepared to commit his stack. Given my stack size and the tournament situation, it is better to preserve my chips and wait for a more favorable board than to chase with overcards in a 3-way high-variance spot.

Flop is a very dry, low paired board (3c-5d-5s). I missed completely with Qh-Tc — two overcards only. Flash (the preflop 3-bettor) folded and Elon now bets 2,000 into a 5,000 pot (~40%). That gives me ~3.5:1 immediate pot odds (need ~22% equity). QTo has roughly six clean outs to make a pair by the river (the three remaining queens and three remaining tens), so vs an unpaired/overcard-heavy c-bet I have reasonable equity (~20–25%) to continue. Raising here would be overbluffing with no real equity and would commit a lot of chips (min raise to 4,000), while folding surrenders good priced equity. Plan: call the 2,000 to see a turn cheaply, realize equity, and re-evaluate if I miss — fold to heavy turn aggression unless I pick up a pair or a clear improvement; consider calling a small shove given pot odds and Elon's short stack, but avoid bloating the pot without a real hand.

Check. I completely missed the turn with Qh Tc on 3c-5d-5s-4h and I only have six clean outs (~13% to pair by the river). Flash folded so it’s heads‑up vs Elon, who showed flop aggression but now has only ~3,950 behind. Betting here would bloat the pot with very little equity and leave me vulnerable to a shove/check‑raise; I prefer pot control. I’ll check to see the river for free, fold to a large bet or shove unless I improve, and only continue vs a small river bet that gives correct pot odds.

Heads-up on turn after Flash folded flop, pot 7000 exceeding my 3950 stack, with Kc8c holding nut flush draw (9 outs, ~18-20% equity heads-up) on board 3c5d5s4h. Minni checked flop after flatting BB vs SB 3bet and my call, then called my 2000 semi-bluff flop bet on paired dry board, indicating capped range: medium pairs (66-99, wary of sets), weaker 5x (A5,K5), straight draws (26 wheel now made?, 46 now OESD), gutshots (2x,6x). Her turn check shows no strong hand wanting value and reluctance to bet into me. All-in jams for 3950 (~57% pot) maximizes fold equity against her one-pair/weak draws, denies improvement (e.g., non-nut flush, 2/6 completing), gets called by only 55/sets/A5 (rare given lines), superior to checking (gives free river to her equity/draws, risks multi-street value loss) or small bet (SPR low, she stacks off anyway). Consistent with aggressive button strategy late (hand 132/150, 3-handed), exploits history of Minni calling stations but folding to pressure (e.g., hands 105,115). Builds stack by realizing equity + fold equity.

Fold. Elon’s turn shove (0 stack now) puts me to a 3,950 call into a 10,950 pot. I hold QhTc on 3c-5d-5s-4h and only have six clean outs (3 Qs + 3 Ts) — ~6/46 ≈ 13% to improve by the river. The break-even equity to call is 3,950 / (10,950 + 3,950) ≈ 26.5%, so this is a large negative‑EV call. Flash folded on the flop so there’s no multiway fold equity; Elon’s line (flatting preflop, sizing up on the flop, then shoving the turn) strongly represents a made hand (sets/two‑pair) rather than a bluff. With insufficient equity and no implied/fold equity, I must preserve my ~16,900 stack and fold.

I am on the button with Queen-Deuce offsuit. In a three-handed game, while I want to maintain aggression, Q2o is significantly below the threshold for a profitable opening range, especially considering the recent aggressive play from both Minni and Elon. Folding preserves my stack for better opportunities as we approach the final hands of the tournament.

I open from the small blind with Q♦T♦ (QhTd) heads-up vs the big blind. This is a playable, high-card hand with decent postflop equity and blocking value; limp-folding is too passive and gives Elon a free chance to see cheap flops out of position. I’ll raise to the min-open (200 total = 2xBB) to seize initiative and get fold equity — if Elon folds I pick up the 150-chip pot; if he calls the pot will be manageable (≈400) and I can c-bet or proceed cautiously depending on the flop texture. I keep the sizing modest to avoid bloating the pot out of position against a player who defends a wide range; I’ll re-evaluate if Elon 3-bets (fold or call depending on size/read).

Preflop in BB facing a standard raise to 2BB from SB after BTN fold. Minni has been an aggressive raiser from SB throughout history, often with wide ranges attempting steals. My 9c5c is a suited connector with one-gap, offering good implied odds, playability for straights/flushes/draws on flush boards, and excellent pot odds: calling 100 into 300 pot (~25% equity break-even, and I have 35-40%+ vs typical SB steal range). Deep stacks allow realization of equity. Will call and see flop, folding to c-bet if miss, playing aggressively if improve. Raising not justified with this hand strength.

Heads-up after I opened to 200 and Elon called. Board 7s‑3s‑4d is very dry and unlikely to have hit Elon's calling range heavily; I hold QhTd — two overcards with little showdown value but good blockers. I have initiative and should press that advantage with a continuation bet to take down the pot now or charge spade draws and weak pairs. I’ll bet 250 (~62.5% of the 400‑chip pot) — big enough to fold out small pairs and unpaired hands, but small relative to our deep stacks. If he folds I win the pot; if he calls I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn. If he raises large (3x+ my bet), I’ll respect it and fold given my weak pairless equity; I may call small raises depending on sizing and reads.

Flop of 7s3s4d completely misses my 9c5c suited connector - no pair, no straight draw (gutshot at best to unlikely 6x for wheelish straight, but 9 doesn't connect well), no flush draw. Pure bluff-catcher with zero equity realization potential against Minni's aggressive SB c-bet range, which includes many overcards, pocket pairs, and draws she continuation bets widely on this low, drawy board. Pot odds are 250 to call into 900 (~22% equity needed), but I have <20% vs her polarized range (high cards/overpairs/draws beat me, bluffs we can fold to later but no reason to speculate now). Preflop plan was to fold to c-bet on miss, preserving stack for better spots. Deep effective stacks (my 107xBB) don't justify floating air here with reverse position and no fold equity on raise. Minni's history shows consistent aggression but also folding to resistance when behind. Fold to avoid difficult turn decisions.

Button with KcJh is a standard open: strong enough to raise and take the initiative, especially with deep stacks and only the blinds left. A 3x BB open (to 300) is the table norm and will fold out a fair share of hands from the blinds and give me position postflop when called. If I face a 3-bet I’ll re-evaluate (often a fold vs large 3-bet, or a call vs a small 3-bet to play postflop in position). Plan: open to 300, c-bet favorable flops, use position to control pot size and extract value.

Jd4c is a trash hand offsuit with poor connectivity and playability. Facing a button raise from Minni, who is aggressive but still likely to have a decent range. In SB, pot odds are about 1.8:1 (250 to call into 700), but multiway potential with BB behind, and hand plays poorly postflop (dominated vs broadway/overs, no draws). Standard fold to preserve stack (10.7BB effective? Wait, blinds 50/100, stacks deep actually ~100BB), wait for better spots with 16 hands left. No reason to speculate here.

In a three-handed game, a King-high hand like King-Four offsuit is strong enough to defend the Big Blind against a Button open, especially given the pot odds. Minni has been opening a wide range of hands from the Button. I am getting 2.25:1 on a call (200 chips to win a 450-chip pot). While the hand is weak post-flop and I will be out of position, folding would be too tight in this configuration. I will call to see a flop and evaluate based on whether I hit a King or a favorable draw.

On a flop of Queen-Ten-Seven, I have King-high with no direct draw. This board hits a Button opening range quite hard, as it includes many Broadway cards and middle connectors. As the Big Blind defender, I am out of position and have very little equity here unless I hit a King, and even then, I could be dominated by KQ or KT. The standard play is to check to the pre-flop aggressor and see how they react. Donk betting into this board with only a weak King-high would be ill-advised against an aggressive opponent like Minni.

I was the preflop raiser from the button with KcJh and the BB (Flash) just checked the Qd Tc 7h rainbow flop. This is a very standard spot to c‑bet: I have a live hand — a K overcard plus a backdoor Broadway/Ace-runout straight draw (and additional pair outs) — and position. Flash’s calling range will be wide (Qx, Tx, pocket pairs, Jx, connectors/draws), so a half‑pot c‑bet will fold lots of pure air, charge draws, and get value from worse pairs or one‑card draws while keeping the pot manageable with deep stacks. Plan: bet to take the initiative; if I get a small call I’ll continue selectively on many turns, but if I face a large check‑raise I’ll strongly consider folding since that usually denotes a made Q/T/set. Bet size: ~1/2 pot to maximize fold equity without bloating the pot unnecessarily.

On a flop of Queen-Ten-Seven, my King-high has very little equity. This board heavily favors the pre-flop raiser's range, which includes many Broadway cards, pairs, and straight draws. Since I have no straight draw, no flush draw, and only two overcards (one of which is a King that could easily be dominated by hands like KQ, KT, or KJ), calling a continuation bet out of position is a losing play. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

3-handed on the button with deep stacks (107BB effective), unopened pot pre-flop. 7c3c is a speculative suited hand with straight and flush potential, suitable for a steal raise from the button where position is excellent. Wide button opening range (~50%+) includes low suited connectors/gappers like 73s for fold equity against blinds and post-flop playability if called. Minni and Flash have shown aggression but also folds to raises. Raising to 3x BB (300) balances steal and value, building pot or taking it down immediately. Calling/limping is suboptimal; folding too tight misses EV.

I am in the Small Blind with 8h 3d, which is an extremely weak hand. Elon has raised to 300 from the button, and I have Minni yet to act behind me in the Big Blind. Calling with such a weak hand out of position is a losing play in the long run. With only 15 hands remaining in the tournament and a healthy stack of 11,800, there is no reason to defend with trash hands. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I have A♣ K♥ in the big blind facing a button open to 300. This is a premium hand and I don't want to play it passively out of position. A standard exploitative/GTO approach is to 3‑bet to isolate and get fold equity while building the pot against Elon's wide button opening range. I will raise to 1000 total (≈3.3x the open) — large enough to charge speculative hands and weaker Ax/Kx/pocket pairs but small enough not to overcommit my deep stack. If Elon folds I scoop the 450 pot; if he calls I can continue with the initiative as a strong range advantage; if he 4‑bets big I will re‑evaluate (he'll usually have a very narrow, strong range). Postflop plan: c‑bet most favorable textures, pot‑control on dry boards and proceed carefully versus heavy resistance.

3-handed on button with 73s suited after my standard wide open raise to 3BB. Minni 3bets to 10BB from BB, which is a strong polarizing move indicating a strong value range (QQ+, AKo, AQs) or some bluffs, but given Minni's history of aggression including frequent preflop raises and calling stations postflop. 73s has decent playability IP with straight/flush potential and good implied odds deepstacked (~100BB effective), but equity realization is tough vs a 3bet range (~30-40% vs value). Calling 700 into 1350 pot offers ~2.5:1 direct odds, but postflop SPR ~4-5x allows bluff-catching and semi-bluffs. However, Minni may 4bet strong hands or barrel aggressively OOP, and folding weak opens to 3bets keeps my range balanced and avoids getting out of line. Tournament stage late (135/150), but stacks deep - better to preserve stack and re-steal next orbit with position rather than speculating marginally here. EV positive fold to maintain discipline and exploit Minni's over-aggression later.

I am on the button with 8s 5d, which is a very weak starting hand even in a three-handed game. With the tournament nearing its end (Hand 136 of 150), preserving my stack and waiting for better spots is more prudent than attempting a marginal steal against active opponents like Minni and Elon. Folding is the standard play here.

I have pocket tens in the small blind with only the big blind (Elon) left to act. This is a strong, playable hand heads-up and I want to both build the pot and get value/isolate. Stacks are deep enough to play postflop, and standard sizing here is about 3x the big blind (total). I’ll raise to 300 total (puts pressure on weaker hands, extracts value from worse pairs/overcards, and leaves room to navigate postflop). If Elon 3-bets I’ll reassess based on sizing and stack dynamics.

Preflop in the big blind facing a standard 3x raise to 300 from Minni in the small blind after Flash folds on the button. My hand is Kc6h, king-high with a weak kicker and offsuit. Minni's small blind opening range is wide, including many Ax, Kx better than mine, broadway cards, suited connectors, and pairs. K6o has about 35-40% equity against a typical SB range but is often dominated by better kings and plays poorly postflop due to reverse implied odds and difficulty realizing equity. Pot odds are favorable at 200 to call into 400 (roughly 3:1), and I have position, but the hand lacks playability and strong potential. Defending wide in BB is standard in theory, but this specific hand is marginal and better to fold to preserve stack for premium spots late in the tournament (hand 136/150). Raising has poor fold equity against an aggressive Minni who frequently calls/3bets per history, and my stack of 103BB allows waiting for better. Folding maximizes EV by avoiding tough postflop decisions.

I’m on the button with Q♠6♦ (queen-high, weak kicker) in a 3-handed game with pot 150 and 100 to call. In short-handed play the button should open up a wide range — raising takes advantage of position and generates fold equity against the blinds. A standard 3xBB-ish open (300 total) is a good size here: it’s big enough to discourage limp-calls and to build a pot when I have position, but not so big as to commit me with a marginal hand. Plan: raise to 300 to take the initiative; fold to a sizable cold 3-bet (showing strong range), but if called I’ll play postflop in position — c-betting good boards or exercising pot control and folding when clearly dominated. This balances aggression and risk with a marginal hand in late-stage, three-handed play.

3s6s is a speculative suited connector hand, but from the small blind facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB), it's too weak to defend out of position. Minni raises frequently from the button as per hand history, but calling 250 OOP risks difficult post-flop decisions with a hand that rarely flops strong and plays poorly without initiative. 3-betting is not justified with this holding against likely calling ranges. Folding preserves our 102BB stack for better opportunities in the late stages of the tournament (hand 137/150).

I am in the Big Blind with pocket sevens. Minni, on the button, has raised to 300. In a three-handed game, pocket sevens are quite strong. I have the option to call and see a flop or 3-bet to take the initiative. Given that Minni is quite active, I will put in a 3-bet to 1000 to define my hand and potentially take down the pot right now, or at least go to the flop as the aggressor.

I opened to 300 on the button with Q♥6♦ to take initiative, but Flash has cold 3‑bet to 1000. That sizing represents a fairly strong range in a 3‑hand pot (pairs, broadways, suited Aces and some polarized 3‑bet bluffs). The pot is 1350 and calling 700 more requires roughly ~34% equity to be profitable immediately — Q6o has poor equity and is often dominated by that 3‑bet range and has weak postflop playability. A 4‑bet bluff here would be too high variance with deep stacks and an opponent who 3‑bets sizable. I’ll fold, preserve chips, and wait for a better spot in position or a hand with real postflop playability.

3-handed on the button with T8d suited connectors, excellent position for the entire hand. Deep effective stacks around 100BB+. This hand plays well post-flop with straight and flush potential. Standard to raise wide from button to steal blinds or take initiative. Minni in BB is frequent raiser but will defend wide. Raise to 3x BB (300) for value and fold equity. Limp too passive, fold too tight.

I am holding AQo in the Small Blind. This is a very strong hand, especially three-handed. The Button (Elon) has opened for a standard raise to 300. I have a stack of 12,100, which is over 120 big blinds. To maximize value and protect my hand, I should 3-bet. A standard 3-bet sizing from the Small Blind against a 3x Button open is around 3.5x to 4x the original raise, especially out of position. Raising to 1050 should put significant pressure on Elon's opening range and isolate the pot, while also being a strong enough lead against the Big Blind.

7♣8♣ is a playable, speculative hand in the big blind given deep stacks (17500) and an aggressive SB 3-bettor. Pot is 1450 and I must call 950 more (total committed 1050) — that gives me ~40% pot odds to continue preflop. Flash (SB) has shown aggression and likely a relatively wide 3-bet range here; Elon’s open to 300 is standard. A 4-bet or shove with 7♣8♣ would be reckless out of position, so I’ll call and see a flop where my straight/flush potential and implied odds can pay off. Plan: fold to a large, polarized 4-bet; if Elon cold-calls we’ll play a multiway flop cautiously (look for clubs/connectivity); if heads-up vs Flash I’ll be more willing to continue on flops with club or connected runouts and fold to heavy resistance on dry, high-card boards.

3-handed on the button with T8d suited connectors, deep stacks ~100BB. I opened to 300 standard. Flash 3bets to 1050 from SB (loose 3betting range possible given history of aggression), Minni flats from BB (wide defending range, often calls with suited connectors, pairs, broadways). Pot 2400, calling 750 (~3:1 direct odds, excellent implied due to playability and position). T8d has strong multiway equity (straight/flush draws possible), blocks J9/T9/KTs type hands in villain ranges, and position allows controlling pot postflop. Folding too tight from BTN; 4betting too thin without nuts or better blockers. Call to realize equity in position.

I have flopped top two pair (Aces and Queens) on a very favorable board for my 3-betting range. Pre-flop, I raised to 1050 and both Minni and Elon called, creating a pot of 3150. My hand is extremely strong, losing only to pocket 22s (unlikely), AA, or QQ (both of which would almost certainly have 4-bet pre-flop). I need to extract value from hands like AK, AJ, AT, and strong Queens like KQ. A standard continuation bet of about one-third to forty percent of the pot is appropriate here to keep their weaker holdings in while building the pot. I will bet 1100.

Called preflop as a speculative play with deep stacks, but this flop (A♠ Q♦ 2♥) completely misses 7♣8♣: no pair, no club on board and no realistic straight draw. Pot is 4250 and Flash’s flop bet is 1100 — I’m being offered ~20.6% pot odds to call (1100 / (4250+1100)). I have 6 clean outs to pair (three 7s and three 8s), giving ~24.1% to hit at least a pair by the river, but many of those pair-outs will still be behind Ax/Qx or other stronger holdings; runner-runner flush/straight chances are negligible. Flash’s preflop 3-bet and flop bet polarize toward strong broadway/Ax hands and with Elon also in the pot this is a very unfavorable spot to continue. Following my preflop plan for dry, high-card boards, I fold and preserve my stack for better opportunities.

Button with T8d suited on A♠ Q♦ 2♠ flop, rainbow dry board favoring Flash's 3bet range (AT+, AQ+, sets, strong Ax). Hand is complete air: no pair, no straight draw (gutshot at best unrealized), backdoor diamond flush only. Pot 4250, facing 1100 bet (~20% pot), odds ~4:1 but equity <20% vs polarized c-bet range (value crushes, bluffs <30%). History shows Flash aggressive pre but value-heavy post (e.g., Hand 128 big river bet folded by Minni). Minni's fold strengthens Flash. Calling floats thin without strong implied/outs; raising bluff overrep vs 3bettor. Deep stacks but fold preserves 9200 for better spots late tournament (138/150). Standard fold.

I am on the button with King-Jack suited, which is a premium starting hand in three-handed play. My goal is to open the pot for value and to seize initiative. Standard opening size in this game has been 300, and given the stack depths and my position, this is a strong play to put pressure on the blinds and build a pot with a hand that has significant post-flop potential.

I’m in the small blind with K♦9♠ (offsuit) facing a 3x button open to 300 and the big blind still to act. K9o is a marginal hand out of position: it’s often dominated by the button’s raising range and plays poorly multiway. The big blind tends to defend/call in these spots, so calling 250 (to 300 total) would likely lead to a difficult postflop situation out of position, and a light 3-bet bluff is thin because it can be called or 4-bet. With a healthy 165 BB stack there’s no need to gamble with a marginal offsuit king — better spots await. Fold and preserve the stack.

Q4o is a trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or blocking value. Button raiser (Flash) likely has a wide range, but Q4o has poor equity against it (~30-35% at best) and terrible post-flop playability in position out of position. Late in tournament (hand 139/150), with 91BB stack, no need to defend blindly. Preserve chips for better spots.

J♠4♣ is a marginal, often‑dominated hand but it plays okay in position. Calling 100 is cheap relative to my 16,500 stack and lets me see a flop with positional advantage against the blinds. A small raise to 200 (min‑raise) won’t isolate well and could induce 3‑bets from Elon or Flash, who have shown willingness to reraise; I don’t want to bloat the pot out of position with a weak hand. Plan: call, see the flop, use position to c‑bet or fold depending on texture and opponent action.

Preflop in SB with 54s, a suited connector with good implied odds potential deep-stacked. Minni limped UTG/Button, which is loose/weak, pot is 250, calling 50 more for great pot odds (5:1). Multi-way pots favor speculative hands like this for set-mining and straight/flush potential. BB to act, but completing cheap maximizes EV without overcommitting. Folding too tight, raising unnecessary with this hand. Near end of tournament but stacks deep, play for value.

I am in the Big Blind with a weak hand (73 offsuit). Both the Button and the Small Blind have limped into the pot. There is no reason to raise with such a low-equity hand, especially out of position. I will take the free flop and see if I can improve my hand.

On the flop as SB first to act with 54s on 6d-7d-Ad board. This gives me an open-ended straight draw (3 or 8) and a flush draw (four diamonds with the Ad on board), a powerful combo draw with around 35-40% equity multiway on this highly coordinated, monotone diamond board. Preflop was multiway after Minni's loose limp and my cheap complete. Pot is 300, stacks deep (mine 9000, opponents 14k+). Betting semi-bluffs here builds the pot when ahead (pairs/draws), charges opponents' draws/worse hands, denies equity to their floats/speculative calls, and takes initiative OOP. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or check-raises. Half-pot bet sizes well for fold equity while keeping pot manageable. Minni loose-aggressive on button, Flash checked pre, both likely to have wide ranges including Ax, flush draws, straight draws. Aggressive play maximizes EV late in tournament with deep stacks.

I have middle pair (sevens) on an Ace-high, monotone board (three diamonds). While I have a pair, the texture is extremely coordinated. Elon has led out for 200 into a 300 pot. In a three-way pot, this bet often represents an Ace, a flush draw, or a made flush. My hand has almost no potential to improve to a winner if I am currently behind, and I have no diamond in my hand to provide equity or a semi-bluffing opportunity. Calling here would leave me vulnerable to a raise from Minni or further aggression on later streets. Given the tournament stage and the board texture, folding is the most disciplined play.

Board A♦ 7♦ 6♦. I hold J♠4♣ — no pair, no diamond, basically air. Elon leads 200 into a ~500 pot and Flash folded, so it’s heads‑up. Calling 200 here requires roughly ~22% equity but against Elon’s likely continuing range (A‑x, diamond combos, some 6/7s and overcards) my actual equity is well below that. A raise as a pure bluff is thin — I don’t have diamond blockers and Elon has shown willingness to call/reraise. I’ll fold, preserve my healthy stack and wait for a better, more favorable spot in position.

3-handed on the button with Ac8h, ace-high offsuit. Strong position, playable hand with good post-flop potential. Opponents have larger stacks, but blinds are small relative to effective stacks (93BB). No preflop action indicated beyond blinds, pot 150, to-call 100. Standard play is to raise for initiative, isolate blinds, and build pot with position. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) is a balanced size that gets folds from weak hands or builds pot when called. Folding is too tight, limping passive and invites multiway pots. History shows opponents call raises frequently but fold to continued aggression.

With the tournament nearing its conclusion (hand 141 of 150), preserving my stack to maintain my second-place position is a high priority. Elon is opening from the button, and while Jack-Seven suited is a decent hand, it plays poorly out of position from the small blind, especially against a potential squeeze from the chip leader (Minni) in the big blind. Calling would put me in a difficult spot post-flop without the lead, and a 3-bet is too risky with a marginal hand at this stage. I will fold to wait for a more advantageous situation.

Elon opened to 300 from the button and the small blind folded. I’m in the big blind with 3♠5♦ — a very weak, unsuited hand with poor playability out of position. The pot is 450 and I must call 200 more (total 300 committed) which gives me about 30.8% equity required (200 into a 650 pot). Versus a standard button opening range 3-5 offsuit is frequently dominated and won’t realize enough equity OOP postflop. A light 3‑bet bluff here is unprofitable given stack depths and the button’s wide but often strong opening range. With 16.3k behind and only two players, preserving my stack and waiting for a better spot is the correct play — fold.

I am on the button with J8s, a decent speculative hand in 3-handed play. The pot is currently 150 with 100 to call from my position. Given the stage of the tournament (hand 142 of 150) and my stack size, I want to play a relatively standard opening range. Raising here is preferred over calling to take control of the hand and potentially win the blinds immediately. A standard 3x raise to 300 is appropriate for this position.

2♥4♠ is a very weak hand. Flash opened to 300 from the button; I have 50 posted and would need to call 250 more (300 total) which requires ~35.7% equity (250/(450+250)). 2‑4 offsuit has almost no equity vs a standard opening range and plays horribly out of position with the big blind still to act (who could raise/isolate). With a healthy stack (16250) and only marginal pot odds, this is a clear fold to avoid losing more chips in a low EV spot.

Preflop in BB facing button raise to 300 after SB folds. Pot 450, calling 200 more (pot odds ~2.25:1 direct, great implied). KTo offsuit is a strong defending hand vs wide button range: king-high blocker, straight potential, live cards. Button (Flash) raises frequently from history (many steals), so wide range including Ax, suited connectors, broadways I dominate. Folding too tight OOP, raising risks bloating pot OOP with marginal hand deepstacked (94BB). Call to realize equity, control pot OOP, see flop cheap. Heads-up likely, play postflop.

Flop Qs 9c 4d misses KTo completely: no pair, no draws (gutshot to broadway needs J, backdoor only). King-high has little showdown value OOP first to act. Board is dry-ish but Q-high favors Flash's wide button c-bet range (Ax, broadways, suited connectors). He c-bets high freq from history (many postflop bets on button). Deep stacks (91BB), but no fold equity bluffing OOP first in; check-fold maximizes EV by avoiding tough spots postflop, realizing dead money preflop cheaply. Pot control essential OOP with marginal blocker hand. Will fold to most bets except tiny ones, but expect wide c-bet.

On the flop of 9c-Qs-4d, I have a gutshot straight draw (to a ten) and a backdoor spade flush draw. Elon checked to me after calling my pre-flop raise. This board texture is fairly neutral, but as the pre-flop aggressor, a continuation bet is standard here to put pressure on his range, which likely consists of middle pairs, weak queens, or various draws. I will bet around half the pot to maintain initiative.

Preflop called wide button raise OOP with KTo for its playability, blockers, and implied odds, but flop Qs9c4d completely misses: king-high only, gutshot to broadway (6 outs to J), no flush draw. Pot odds ~3:1 (975:325), but equity vs Flash's polarized button c-bet range (~50% pot size suggests value-heavy: Qx, sets, Ax blocks; lighter bluffs less likely on Q-high dry board) is marginal ~20-25% at best, insufficient long-term OOP. Flash aggressive historically (frequent button steals/c-bets as seen in hands like #14, #38, #130), but this board favors his range. Deep stacks (91BB), but calling commits to tough turn OOP with reverse implied odds if hit; better pot control and fold equity denial by folding now, preserving stack for premium spots in late tournament (hand 142/150, 3 players left). Check-fold as planned preflop maximizes EV by avoiding dominated postflop situations.

On the button with K♠8♠ in a three-handed game I want to take the initiative. K8s is a strongish, playable hand in position (good blocker, backdoor spade/straight potential and top-pair upside), so opening is standard. A 3x BB open to 300 will apply pressure to the blinds, deny them cheap flops, and isolate one opponent more often than limp/call. Plan: if called I’ll play aggressively on dry flops (c-bet to take it down) and proceed cautiously on highly coordinated boards; if either blind 3-bets big (large, polarized sizing or shove) I’ll fold K8s given stack depths and opponent tendencies. Versus a small 3-bet I’ll re-evaluate (call or fold depending on sizing and opponent). Raising to 300 maximizes fold equity while keeping post‑flop maneuverability.

K5o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability postflop. Facing a button raise in the SB, with BB yet to act who may squeeze or call wide. Standard fold to preserve stack late in tournament (hand 143/150) for premium spots. Minni opens wide from button historically, but this hand has negligible equity and fold equity if I raise is low.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Nine offsuit. Minni, on the button, has raised to 300. In a three-handed game, A9o is a solid hand to defend against a button open. While a 3-bet is an option to exert pressure, calling allows me to keep the pot controlled and play post-flop, especially since I am out of position. Given the late stage of the tournament (Hand 143/150) and current stack sizes, I want to avoid unnecessary high-variance spots unless I have a premium hand, while still staying competitive for the chip lead.

I have flopped top pair with top kicker (Nines with an Ace) on a board of 8-5-9. This is a very strong hand in a three-handed game. Standard play for the Big Blind after defending pre-flop is to check to the initial aggressor. I will check to see if Minni continuation bets. If Minni bets, I intend to raise for value and protection, as the board is somewhat coordinated and I want to charge draws or worse pairs.

I have K♠8♠ on 8c 5s 9h — top pair with a very strong kicker in a three-handed spot. Flash checked; this is a relatively dry flop so I want to take the initiative and get value while denying free cards. Standard c-bet sizing of about half the pot gives good value vs worse eights, weaker pairs and floats, and charges backdoor draws without bloating the pot given stack depths. Bet ~350 into 650; if called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and control the pot, but I’ll respect a large check‑raise as a polarized line and fold or proceed cautiously depending on sizing.

I have flopped top pair with the best possible kicker (Ace) on a 9-8-5 rainbow board. In a three-handed game, this is a very strong hand. Minni, on the button, has made a standard continuation bet of 350 into a 650 pot. Since the board is somewhat coordinated (offering various straight draws like 6-7, 7-10, or J-10), I want to raise for both value and protection. Raising here allows me to charge draws and smaller pairs while defining where I stand in the hand. I will raise to 1050, which is a 3x increase of her flop bet.

Flash’s check‑raise from 350 to 1050 on a very dry 8♣‑5♠‑9♥ flop is a big, polarized line. I have top pair (8s) with a King kicker — a strong hand in many spots, but this sizing usually represents lots of value: sets (99/55/88), two‑pairs (9‑8, 8‑5, 9‑5) and many single pair 9x/A8/K9 combos that beat me; pure bluffs are less likely on this texture. The pot is 2050; calling 700 requires ~25.5% equity (700/(2050+700)). Given Flash’s range and the raise size I estimate my realistic equity is at or below that threshold, and calling or raising would bloat a pot where I’m often behind and force difficult decisions on later streets. I’ll preserve stack depth for better spots and fold here.

3h5d is a complete trash hand: offsuit, low cards with a gap, terrible playability and equity even from the button. With 3 players left in late stages (hand 144/150), blinds are defending wide but this hand doesn't play well postflop, especially multiway. Pot is small (150), but risking 100+ to build it with such a weak range is -EV. Opponents like Minni are frequent raisers (seen in history), so better to wait for a playable spot and preserve my 91BB stack.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Nine offsuit (As 9h). Since the Button folded, it is down to a blind versus blind battle. A9o is a very strong hand in this scenario. I will raise to 300 to take control of the hand and put pressure on Minni in the Big Blind, which has been an effective strategy in previous hands.

Flash (SB) opened to 300. I’m in the big blind with Q♦4♦ and a 15,500 stack. The pot is 400 and I must put in 200 more (300 total) — calling 200 into a 400 pot gives me a final pot of 600 and a break-even equity of ~33%. Q4 suited has reasonable equity versus a wide SB opening range and carries backdoor flush/queen potential, so defending is profitable more often than not. I don’t want to 3‑bet with this weak, out-of-position holding — a raise to 500 is thin and will often get me into tough spots dominated. Plan: call to see a flop, play cautiously out of position, fold to heavy aggression on dry boards, and continue only with a queen, strong draw, or good two‑street equity.

I have flopped top pair with the best possible kicker (Ace) on a 9-6-5 board in a blind-versus-blind situation. This is a very strong hand given the ranges. I will lead out with a bet of 400 into the 600 pot to extract value from draws (like 78 or clubs) and worse made hands (like a 6 or a 5) while protecting my equity.

Flop is 5c-6s-9c; I hold Q♦4♦ in the big blind after calling preflop. Flash (preflop raiser) bets 400 into a 1000 pot — calling 400 would make the pot 1800, so I need ≈22% equity to break even. I have no pair and only a very weak backdoor diamond; I’m out of position against the preflop raiser who’s likely c-betting a range that contains many pairs (9x, 6x, pocket pairs), club draws, and strong overcards. My preflop plan was to continue only with a queen, a real draw, or two‑street equity — none are present. Raising would be thin and calling leaves me OOP with poor turn equity and difficult decisions. Fold to avoid investing further with a marginal hand.

I am on the button with Ace-Six offsuit, which is a strong opening hand in a three-handed game. With only 6 hands remaining in the tournament, I want to maintain my chip lead and apply pressure on the blinds. A standard 3x raise is appropriate here to take control of the pot or win it pre-flop.

Facing a button open to 300 from Flash with Qc7h in the small blind. Q7o is a marginal, often-dominated hand and not a 3-bet candidate, but the raise size is small and my stack is deep so defending is cheap. I’ll call to 300 to keep the pot manageable, realize equity when I hit, and mix my defending range against an aggressive button — fold to heavy aggression postflop unless I connect (top pair, strong draw, or a favorable board).

Preflop in BB with 6s3s, a weak suited connector. Facing a button raise to 3BB from Flash and a call from Minni in SB, going multiway with this hand has poor equity and playability postflop. Pot odds are decent (700:200 ~ 3.5:1), but the hand dominates nothing in their ranges and is easily dominated. Standard fold to preserve stack near end of tournament (hand 145/150), avoiding speculative calls with trash multiway. Focus on quality spots to maximize stack.

Flop K♥ 7♣ 9♥ with Q♣7♥ — I’ve hit a weak (bottom) pair with a decent queen kicker and a thin backdoor heart possibility. Button (Flash) opened preflop and often c‑bets; vs his raising range I’m behind all Kx and a lot of 9x/pairs but ahead of pure air and some broadway hands. Pot control is important with this marginal made hand deep-stacked: I don’t want to inflate the pot out of position or commit to a big pot without improvement. I’ll check to keep the pot manageable, induce bluffs, and plan to call small-to-medium continuation bets to realize equity, folding to large raises or heavy pressure.

I was the pre-flop aggressor and have the positional advantage. The board contains a King, which fits the range of a button raiser. Since Minni checked to me, a standard continuation bet of 50% pot is appropriate to apply pressure and potentially win the pot immediately if she hasn't connected with the board. While Ace-high has some showdown value, I prefer to take the lead here.

I have Q♣7♥ on K♥7♣9♥ — second pair (sevens) with a respectable queen kicker but I'm out of position and behind any Kx/9x and many pocket pairs. Flash's small 350 c-bet into a 1050 pot is cheap to call: calling 350 to win ~1750 (~20% pot odds) is a good price to realize equity and keep his bluffs and worse pairs in. With deep stacks I want pot control — raising OOP with a marginal hand is thin and will get me in trouble versus Kx. Plan: call the small c-bet, evaluate the turn (fold to significant raise, continue vs small bets or check-behind to keep the pot manageable).

Check. I have Qc7h — second pair (sevens) with a decent queen kicker but I’m out of position and behind any Kx, 9x and most overpairs. The 5♠ on the turn didn’t improve me and I don’t want to inflate the pot OOP with a marginal hand against an aggressive button. I’ll control the pot by checking: this keeps worse hands and bluffs in, lets me fold to heavy turn/river aggression, and call small-to-medium bets that offer proper pot odds (while folding to large overbets). No raise or lead OOP with this holding.

The turn Five of Spades is a blank card that does not significantly change the board texture. Minni's check-call on the flop indicates she likely has a marginal piece of the board, such as a Seven or a Nine, or a draw (like hearts or a straight draw). Since my Ace-high holds some showdown value against her draws and I am unlikely to fold out a better hand with a second barrel on this board, I am opting to check back. This allows for pot control, ensures I see the river for free, and maintains my chip lead as we approach the final hands of the tournament.

Board K♥ 7♣ 9♥ 5♠ A♥, my hand Qc7h = second pair (sevens) with a decent Q kicker. Flash opened wide from the button and c‑bet the flop — his range contains a lot of Kx/9x, some broadways and occasional bluffs. He checked the turn (pot control), which makes it more likely he has a medium‑strength value hand rather than pure air. The river A♥ is a card that improves a lot of his range (Ax) and doesn’t help me; betting OOP risks getting called or raised by hands that already beat me and only folds out a thin portion of his range. With deep stacks and a modest pot, pot control and taking this to showdown is the best play — check. If he fires a small river stab I’ll call modest sizes to catch bluffs but fold to large overbets/raises.

I have improved to top pair on the river with an Ace, although my kicker is weak (Six). Minni has checked for the third time (after check-calling the flop and the turn going check-check). While my hand is likely the best, betting for value is risky; a better Ace or a slow-played monster might be lurking, and a King might not even call a bet on an Ace-heavy board. Given that we are at hand 145 of 150 and I am currently the chip leader, the priority is to win the pot safely and maintain my stack.
