Checking back ensures I win the showdown against missed draws or weaker pairs without risking a check-raise.

I raise to 300 (3x the BB). On the Button with Jd7d I have position and a suited Jack with decent playability and backdoor flush potential. Versus two blinds who defend wide, a standard 3x open exploits fold equity and lets me play the hand in position. The size is a tiny portion of my 14.65k stack so the risk is low — I’ll fold to a large 3-bet, but if called I’ll c-bet favorable flops (diamonds or jack-high textures) and play pot-control when I miss.

89s is a strong suited connector with excellent post-flop playability, especially multi-way with deep stacks (89bb effective). Facing a button raise from Minni, who has been an aggressive raiser throughout the history, the pot odds are favorable: calling 250 more into a 450 pot (plus BB action) offers over 2:1 direct odds, and implied odds are high given playability for straights, flushes, and two-pair combos. Folding from SB is too tight against a wide button range. 3-betting as a bluff is possible but risks bloating the pot OOP against a calling station like Minni. Calling keeps the pot smaller, realizes equity well, and allows seeing a flop in position dynamics with Flash BB to act. Near end of tournament (146/150), preserving stack while playing premium speculative hands maximizes EV.

I am holding JTo in the Big Blind. Minni opened from the Button and Elon called from the Small Blind. I am getting excellent pot odds (200 to call into a pot of 700) to see a flop. With only 5 hands left in the tournament and being the current chip leader, I want to play solidly but not fold away equity with a hand as strong as Jack-Ten offsuit. Calling closes the action and allows me to see a flop in a three-way pot without bloating it unnecessarily with a 3-bet.

Flop Qs4cKd gives me a strong spade flush draw (9 outs, ~35% equity heads-up, solid multiway) with 8s9s suited connectors. No pair or straight draw, but live cards and backdoor potential. Multiway pot (900), deep stacks (87bb effective), OOP as SB first to act. Minni's button raise pre-flop is wide/aggressive per history; Flash BB call defensive. Checking risks free card to draws or check-raise, but pot control OOP. Semi-bluff bet optimal: fold equity vs air/marginal (KQxx, weak K/A high), value from worse (Ax flush draws, Qx, low pairs), protection/deny equity. Size 2/3 pot (600) balances, builds pot for nut potential. Near end (146/150), exploiting playability maximizes stack EV over passive play.

On the flop of Qs-4c-Kd, I have an open-ended straight draw with Jh-Tc (8 outs: any Ace or Nine). Elon, the small blind, has led out for 600 into a 900-chip pot. This 'donk lead' into the pre-flop aggressor (Minni) and the big blind (me) usually indicates a strong hand like a King, Queen, or perhaps two pair. However, I am getting pot odds of 2.5:1 (600 to win 1500). My equity with an OESD is roughly 32% to hit by the river and 17% to hit on the turn. While the immediate odds are slightly short for the turn alone, the implied odds are significant if I hit my straight against hands like KQ or AK, which my opponents are likely to hold given the action. Additionally, I am the chip leader and want to maintain my lead without taking unnecessary risks. Calling allows me to see a turn card relatively cheaply while closing the action if Minni folds, or building a larger pot if she also calls.

I opened preflop with Jd7d for position and playability, but the flop Qs-4c-Kd is very unfavorable. I have no pair and only a backdoor diamond nut‑flush possibility that requires both turn and river (very slim ~4%), plus ~6 pair-outs (~24% to pair by the river) — raw improvement odds look marginal, but this is a three‑way pot and both opponents have shown strength (a bet and a call). Pot odds to call 600 into a 2100 pot are about 22%; against two players’ continuing ranges my effective equity is noticeably lower (and if I do pair a jack or a seven I can easily be second‑best vs Kx/Qx). Raising as a bluff here is too risky multiway and likely to be called by better hands. I’ll preserve my stack and fold to this flop aggression.

On the turn 3h, the board is Qs4cKd3h, unimproved for my 8s9s which still holds a strong 9-out spade flush draw (~19% to river) plus live overcards and backdoor straight possibilities (gutshot elements with 3-4-8-9). No pair, but significant equity (~35-40% vs typical calling ranges like Kx, Qx, pocket pairs, weaker draws). Pot 2100, OOP first to act vs Flash (BB defender who called flop semi-bluff bet, range: KQ/AK/AQs/Kxs, pairs 55-QQ/JJ-TT, flush draws, sets unlikely). I rep strength with flop donk/continuation sizing into multiway pot; turn bet maintains pressure, denies equity to his floats/draws, builds for nut flush/TPTK showdowns, exploits fold equity against marginal made hands (KQ, AJs, 88-99, missed draws). Checking risks giving free card to his equity or check-raise jams, while passive induces bluffs I can call. Half-pot bet (1050) thin value/protection, but sizing up to 1300 (60% pot) polarizes range convincingly, maximizes fold equity while pot-committal if raised (my stack 8100 allows jam if shoved). Late tournament (146/150), aggressive line extracts max EV from playability vs passive BB tendencies seen in history (frequent calls but folds to aggression post-flop). Betting optimal.

On the turn, the 3h is a total brick. Elon has continued his aggression with a bet of 1300 into a 2100 pot, which is a significant sizing (about 62% of the pot). I have an open-ended straight draw (8 outs). The probability of hitting my straight on the river is approximately 17.4%. My pot odds are 1300 / 4700, which is about 27.6%. Since my equity (17.4%) is considerably lower than the pot odds (27.6%), this call is not immediately profitable based on pot odds alone. To make the call profitable, I would need to realize significant implied odds on the river. However, with only 5 hands left in the tournament and currently holding the chip lead (15,450 vs Minni's 14,350), it is more prudent to protect my stack rather than gamble on a draw where I am a significant underdog. Losing another 1300 here would put me behind Minni for the lead, whereas folding allows me to maintain a slim lead and wait for better spots in the final few hands.

3-handed on the button in an unopened pot with Ts6s suited connectors. Excellent position for stealing blinds and playing postflop. Hand has good implied odds, straight/flush potential, and blockers. Opponents have large stacks but history shows they fold to button raises often. Raise to 3x BB standard size to build pot or take it down immediately. Near end of tournament (hand 147/150), applying pressure maximizes EV.

With J3s in the Small Blind against a Button raise from Elon, I am looking at a hand that is generally too weak to defend out of position. We are approaching the end of the tournament (hand 147 of 150), and I have a healthy stack of 15,400, which is the current chip lead among the three remaining players. Defending with a marginal hand like J3s against a standard 3x raise is unnecessary and likely to lead to a difficult post-flop situation. I will preserve my stack and wait for better opportunities in the final few hands.

I'm in the big blind with pocket deuces (2c2d) facing a button open to 300. Stacks are deep (~142bb effective). Standard, +EV play with a small pair here is to set-mine: call the extra 200 (commit 300 total) to see a flop. Calling keeps the pot controlled and gives excellent implied odds if I hit a set. A 3-bet would bloat the pot out of position with a hand that plays poorly postflop, and folding is too tight given the price. Plan: see the flop, play cautiously if I miss (check/fold to heavy aggression), and extract maximum value if I connect.

I flopped a set of deuces (2c2d with 2h on board) — an extremely strong hand. The board has two clubs, so villain can have club draws (two clubs in hand = a 4-card flush needing one club), and there are a few combos (77, 33) that beat me, but those are uncommon. With the raiser (Elon) likely wide/aggressive and stacks deep, my objective is to charge draws and get value from worse pairs and hands that will call. A bet of ~0.6 pot (400 into 650) accomplishes protection and value: it prices out pure air, charges club draws, and builds the pot when I'm likely ahead. Plan: continue extracting vs calls on the turn; if he raises large I will re-evaluate (but will be inclined to continue given the strength of a set).

Ts6s completely misses the 7c3c2h flop: no pair, no flush draw (no spades on board), no open straight draw, just overcard Ten and backdoor potential. Pure air with some high card live cards and suited connectors that missed. However, in position on the button vs BB donk-bet of 400 into 1050 (pot odds 3.6:1 or ~22% equity required). Minni's BB calling range vs button raise is wide: Ax suited, low pairs, suited connectors, broadway blockers. Donk-bet sizing ~38% pot on dry low board suggests polarization - value (sets, two-pair, overpairs) or bluffs (missed draws, floats). History shows Minni calls frequently but folds to continued aggression postflop often (e.g., Hand 50, 76, etc.). Deep stacks (9900 effective), late tournament (147/150), blinds tiny relative to stacks. Calling cheap maintains pot control, realizes thin equity (live Ten blocks strong hands like T9/T8), allows turn control and bluff opportunities on blank turns. Raising as bluff has merit (fold equity high on scary board for bluffs), but minraise to 800 is small and invites call; larger raise risks deep stack commitment with air. Folding is nitty - dead money and position worth a cheap float. Plan: call, evaluate turn (bet if blank/improved board or check to pot control). Maximizes EV long-term.

Turn 7h is a dangerous card — my flopped set (I bet 400, Elon called) is now vulnerable to any 7 in his range (single-7 combos make trips; pocket 77 or 33, while rarer, also beat me). Elon’s button opening range and his flop call include many hands that now improve, while at the same time there are plenty of club draws and weaker pairs that will still bet or bluff. I want to keep the pot under control and induce bets from bluffs/weaker hands rather than bloating the pot and committing myself to a tough decision vs a likely made 7. Check to control the size of the pot (pot = 1450, effective stacks ~9500), then call reasonable-sized bets to extract from draws/weaker pairs and fold to large overbets that strongly represent trips/quads.

Turn 7h pairs the top card, creating a paired board with potential flush draws (clubs) and possible straight draws. Minni's flop donk-bet followed by turn check suggests weakness: either capped value (weak pairs, bottom pair), missed draws, or trapping very strong hands unlikely given preflop call range. My Ts6s remains air but with a live Ten (blocks Tx pairs), spade suit for flush rep, and position. Pot 1450, deep stacks (~9500 eff), late tournament. Checking behind risks giving free card to draws or showdown where I lose; betting exploits fold equity as Minni has folded to postflop aggression frequently (e.g., Hands 50, 76, 116). Half-pot bet sizes fold out 70-80% of her checking range (Axo, low pairs, missed suited connectors) while denying equity. Plan: bet 700, c/r river if villain shows strength or check-fold to bets. Maximizes EV by taking pot ~60% time via folds.

I have a disguised set (2c2d) on 7c-3c-2h-7h. I checked the turn to control pot size and induce; Elon’s 700 (~0.48 pot) after calling the flop is a sizing consistent with clubs, weaker pairs, floats, or a protection/value bet with a 7. Raising here would fold out the very hands I want to extract value from (club draws and weaker pairs) and would dangerously bloat the pot against the narrower combos that actually beat me (7x/33). Folding is too tight given my strong hand. Calling 700 keeps draws and worse hands in, preserves my ability to re-evaluate on the river, and extracts value without overcommitting. Plan: call the 700, see the river, and fold only to lines/overbets that strongly represent trips/quads; otherwise extract thin value on a safe river card.

Board paired the 7 on the river and my two-deuce hand turned into 7s full of 2s (best 5: 7c-7h-7s-2c-2d). That is a very strong hand but also one of the weakest full houses here: any opponent holding a 3 (x3) or any pocket pair above deuces (33,44,…,AA) now makes a higher full house than mine, and any 7 in hand would be quads. Elon’s line (open preflop, calling my flop bet, then raising the turn after the 7) is exactly the sort of line pocket pairs or a 7/3 combo would take. Betting the river just builds a bigger pot for a large number of hands that actually beat me; it will mostly get called by better full houses/quads or fold out hands I beat. Pot control and showdown are the prudent play: check and see if he fires a bluff or a small value bet. If he bets a small/medium sized amount I can call; if he jams a big overbet I should fold given how many hands beat me. Therefore I will check behind and take the showdown line rather than bloating the pot.

River 7s triples the 7s on board (7c/7h/7s with 3c/2h), creating a nightmarish texture: nuts are quads (77/7x), full houses (33/22/32/73/72). Frequent checks from Minni (turn/river) after flop donk and turn check-call suggest medium strength: likely mid 7x (A7-K7 weaker end like 76s-97s from BB calling range), low pairs (44-66 unlikely to donk), or capped value. My Ts6s plays as 777-T6 kicker trips (beats 7x with <T kicker like 76-96, Q5 low pairs, Ax floats, missed clubs/straight draws). Live T blocks strong 7T/JT. Position, deep stacks (eff ~8800), tiny blinds late tournament (147/150). Her check invites value bets from strong 7x/FH. History: Minni calls stations but folds aggression (e.g., H50,76,116,121). Pot 2850, bet 2/3 pot (1900) values thin vs her ~30-40% calling range worse kicker/draws, high fold equity vs air (~50% range). Gets calls from 86s,76s,65s etc., folds better on scary board. EV+ vs check (wins small pots uncontested sometimes but misses value). Plan: bet 1900, fold to raise (polarized to monsters we lose to). Maximizes stack pressure 3-handed.

I have 2c2d on 7c-3c-2h-7h-7s — full house: 7s full of 2s. This is a very strong hand but is beaten by any pocket pair 33+ (which make a better full house) and by any 7 in hand (quads). I checked the river to control the pot and induce; Elon’s line (open, call flop, bet turn, bet 1900 into 4750 on the river) is a small/medium sizing consistent with bluffs, club floats, or worse full houses — not a shove that would polarize to only candies that crush me. Calling 1900 commits a relatively small chunk for ~28.6% pot odds (1900 into a 6650 pot after call) and will catch many of his bluffs/weaker pairs; raising would fold out exactly the hands I want to extract value from and only get called by better full houses/quads. I’ll call the 1900; if he jams or makes a large overbet I’ll fold to that increased polarization.

I am on the button with 7h 6c. This is a marginal hand even in a three-handed game. With only three hands left in the tournament (Hands 148, 149, and 150), I am currently in second place, trailing Minni by approximately 2,250 chips. My goal is to maximize my stack while minimizing unnecessary risk. Elon (Big Blind) has recently shown aggression in Hands 146 and 147, and Minni (Small Blind) is the chip leader. Entering a pot with a weak hand like 76 offsuit out of position against either player's defense range is not ideal. I will fold to preserve my stack and look for a better spot in the final two hands to potentially overtake the lead.

I’m in the small blind with A♣? A♥ J♦ (A♠ J♦) — a strong, high-card hand but out of position. Pot is tiny (150) and I only need 50 more to see a flop; folding is too weak and limping/calling leaves me OOP to a wide big-blind defense. With deep stacks I want to take the initiative and either steal the pot preflop or play a single opponent postflop with fold equity. I’ll raise to the minimum allowed (to 200) — it’s a compact, standard raise that isolates Elon’s wide range, preserves maneuverability OOP, and keeps the pot manageable. If Elon 3-bets large I’ll re-evaluate (call moderately sized 3-bets for implied odds, fold to extreme overbets); if he calls I’ll c-bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously when I miss. Total commitment on this action: 200.

Q2 offsuit is a trash hand with poor playability, dominated by Minni's likely wide but strong raising range from the small blind. Facing a raise to 200 total (pot 300, call 100 gives ~3:1 odds), but post-flop OOP with this hand is disastrous. No straight/flush potential, bad implied odds. I'm at 68BB deep, tournament near end (148/150), better to preserve stack for better spots rather than defend marginally. Standard fold.

I’m on the button with 9♠8♥, deep stacks (17800) and only the SB and BB to beat. This is a playable, position-dependent hand with good straight potential; I don’t want to limp and surrender initiative. I’ll open to a standard 3BB (300 total) to apply pressure, pick up the blinds, and keep the pot manageable if I face resistance. If I get a large 3-bet (a sizable 4x+ or shove), I’ll fold this marginal offsuit; if called I’ll use position to play postflop (c-bet small on favorable textures, fold to heavy action when board is hostile). 300 is a size consistent with the table and gives the right balance of fold equity and leeway postflop.

Hand 149 of 150, in SB with J9o offsuit facing a button raise to 300 (250 to call). J9o is a marginal offsuit hand that plays poorly out of position post-flop against a wide button range from Minni, who has been aggressive historically. Pot odds are decent (~2.8:1), but implied odds are poor OOP with this hand, which doesn't make strong hands often and is dominated by better broadway. No reason to speculate here with stacks at 67BB effective; better to preserve stack for the final hand or avoid tough spots. Standard fold.

Hand #149 of 150. I am holding 7d 4c in the Big Blind. Minni raised from the Button. While I am getting decent odds to call the extra 200 chips, 74o is an extremely weak hand. Given that we are on the penultimate hand of the tournament and I currently hold a solid second-place stack, there is no strategic reason to defend with garbage and risk losing more chips post-flop. I will fold and wait for the final hand.

3-handed on the button with TsJs, a strong suited connector with great playability, position, and potential for straights/flushes. Pot is 150, BB 100, calling is +EV due to odds (1.5:1), but raising steals blinds frequently or builds pot OOP when they defend. Standard 3x raise to 300. Opponents deep-stacked but shown to fold to raises (e.g., Minni folds often pre/rarely calls big). Last hand, aggressive play maximizes stack EV by winning dead money or setting up postflop advantage.

This is the final hand of the tournament. I am currently in 2nd place with 15,250 chips, while Minni has 17,850 and Elon has 6,450. I am holding 6c 2c in the Small Blind. Elon has raised from the Button. While 6-2 suited has some minimal potential, it is a very weak hand to play against a Button open and a potentially aggressive Big Blind on the last hand. To overtake Minni for 1st place, I would need to win a substantial pot, which is unlikely with this hand. Folding preserves my stack at 15,200 and secures my 2nd place finish without risking a significant loss on a marginal gamble.

Heads-up after the small blind folded, Ac9c in the big blind is a clear defend. Calling 200 more (total commitment 300) gives good pot odds (200 to win ~450) and keeps a playable, suited-A hand in the pot against what looks like a fairly wide BTN opening range. A small min-3bet to 500 would be too light to fold out many hands and a larger 3bet bloats the pot out of position; calling preserves stack depth (SPR ~9–10) and allows us to realize equity and play position-agnostic postflop lines. I’ll call and see a flop.

I have A♣9♣ on 6♥ Q♣ 7♣ — the nut flush draw plus an ace overcard. Out of position vs the BTN opener, the standard +EV line is to check and see a c-bet: we have ~35% to hit the flush by the river (9 club outs) and ~45% if you include the three remaining aces, so we get good equity to call normal c-bets and don’t want to bloat the pot OOP by betting ourselves. Checking also allows a check‑raise semi‑bluff if Elon fires a very small c‑bet, or a cheap call to realize equity against a wide BTN c‑bet frequency. Plan: check now, call reasonable flop bets (or check‑raise small bets), and if he checks back consider barreling turn on favorable cards (a club or an ace) or continuing as appropriate.

Heads-up on flop with TsJs after our preflop button raise. Board 6h-Qc-7c is coordinated with club flush draw, possible straights (5-8), and pairs. We have overcards (T,J block some Qx,Jx), backdoor spade flush and straight draws, excellent playability, and position. Minni called pre OOP (wide range: pairs, suited connectors, Ax suited clubs likely), checked (weak or trapping). Pot 650, deep stacks. C-bet for value from worse Ax/high cards/weak Qx, fold equity vs trash (high, Minni folds postflop often e.g., hands 5,20,24,29 partial), protection vs draws. Size 400 (~60% pot) balances value/bluff, denies equity, builds on preflop aggression. Last hand (#150), maximize EV by taking it now or setting up river shove if villain floats.

I have A♣9♣ on 6♥‑Q♣‑7♣: the nut flush draw plus an ace overcard. Elon’s 400 into the ~650 pot (pot now 1050) gives me to‑call 400. I have 9 club outs (~35% to hit by the river) and three extra ace outs (≈45% by river total), so my equity comfortably exceeds the break‑even threshold (400/(1050+400) ≈ 27.6%). Raising OOP as a semi‑bluff is an option, but it bloats the pot out of position and risks a strong reraise from an aggressive opener; calling realizes equity, keeps SPR manageable versus Elon’s smaller stack, and preserves fold/river maneuverability. Plan: call the 400 now (total commitment 700). If I hit a club or an ace on the turn I’ll apply pressure; if I miss a clean card I’ll re‑evaluate vs his turn action.

Check. I hold A♣9♣ with the nut club draw and an ace kicker, but the turn (J♥) is not a “favorable” club/ace card to barrel on. Elon checked the turn after c-betting the flop, which suggests a lot of his range is now medium-strength hands that will either call a bet or check back, so betting OOP here would bloat the pot and get called by many hands that currently dominate our draw. With one card to come our improvement chances are only ~25–27%, so I prefer to control the pot, realize equity and keep options for the river. Plan: check down this turn; if the river gives a club or an ace I’ll bet for value/pressure, otherwise re-evaluate a river bluff vs checking behind to avoid committing to marginal spots.

Turn card Jh pairs our Jack, giving us second pair (JJ) with Ten kicker and spade blocker on a coordinated 6h-Qc-7c-Jh board (clubs flush draw, 8-9 straight). OOP villain's preflop call (loose from BB), flop check-call vs our c-bet (range: Qx, 76s/65s, sets, clubs/Ax suited, 89 suited), and turn check suggests medium strength (weak Qx, missed draws, mid pairs) rather than nuts (would bet for value/protect). We block QJ/TJ/Jx draws/value, ahead of Ax high/clubs/88-TT/98o often. Deep stacks (6050 eff), pot 1450; bet 2/3 pot (1000) extracts thin value from worse (Ax clubs, 99-TT, QTo), protection vs river clubs/straights/OTD, fold equity vs trash/floats (Minni folds to turn aggression frequently, e.g., hands 56,76,105,116,130). Checking concedes pot OOP or gives free cards to 15+ outs; bet maximizes EV in position last hand, setting up river if check-raised or big pot if called. Consistent with preflop/flop aggression.

I have A♣9♣ on 6♥ Q♣ 7♣ J♥ — the nut club draw plus an ace overcard. Elon’s turn bet of 1,000 into the ~2,450 pot gives me to‑call 1,000 for roughly 29% pot odds (1,000 / (2,450+1,000) ≈ 29%). I have about 12 nominal outs (9 clubs + 3 aces) → ~26% raw equity to improve by the river, so a pure call on raw odds is slightly below break‑even. Raising OOP is a semi‑bluff alternative, but it bloats the pot out of position, invites large 3‑bets or a shove from an aggressive opponent who has shown willingness to call/commit, and likely won’t fold a large enough portion of his range to make a small raise clearly +EV. I’ll call the 1,000 to realize equity while keeping the pot manageable. Plan: if the river brings a club or an ace I’ll bet for value/pressure; if the river is a blank I’ll check and re‑evaluate vs his action (fold to large river aggression unless pot odds/read justify a bluff). If he jams now after my action I’ll re‑evaluate given shove size and his range, calling only when the pot odds and my read make it profitable.

I have the nut club flush (A♣9♣) on a 6♥ Q♣ 7♣ J♥ T♣ board — essentially unbeatable (flush beats straights/trips here). Elon checked the river after betting earlier streets, so he likely holds medium-strength hands (Kx with a club, single pair, trips) or a second-best flush. Checking behind surrenders value; shoving can fold out some worse flushes/pairs. A value bet around 2/3 pot will extract calls from K‑club combos, weaker flushes, and many pairs that will still call, while not being so large that he folds everything. Pot is 3450 — bet ~2300 to get value but keep calls likely. If Elon jams, I can call because my nut flush is ahead of his realistic range. Action: value bet ~2/3 pot to maximize value.

River 10c♣ pairs our Ts for two pair (Jacks and Tens, blocking QJs/QTs/JTs), on a board completing club flush draws and 8-9 straights (6-7-8-9-10). Pot 3450, villain (Minni BB) donks 2300 (~2/3 pot) after OOP check-calls 400 flop + 1000 turn (1400 total invested). Her range: strong (♣ flush A♣/K♣/Q♣x, 89 straight, QT/QJ two pair > us, sets QQ/77/66), but passive line pre-river suggests not monsters earlier (would raise c-bets). We block key value (Js/Ts), ahead of Ax♣ weak flush/A-high, missed draws turned bluff (rare but history shows occasional thin calls). Pot odds 2300 to win 5750 (~2.5:1, 28% equity needed); two pair >50% vs polarized value/bluff range. Raise: low FE (she calls nuts/QT/QJ), risks full 5050 stack (5050 eff); fold: too tight, misses showdown value/bluffs. Call realizes equity, pot-committal, maximizes final stack EV (10700 if win, 2750 if lose > folding to 5050). Aggro image paid off preflop/flop/turn; now value extract/call down.
